turns.markdown:
## Turn 4 - sumi (player) - character-creation, character-creation

# Turn 4 — Sumi

*[Sumi sets down a thermos of tea on the table, takes the lid off slowly,
and waits until everyone's looking before she speaks. She has a half-page
of notes in her own hand, in pencil, and she's already half-erased one
line and rewritten it.]*

**Sumi (OOC):** Okay. ...Hold on.

...

I want this to be one I can stay with. So I went looking for a
non-adjacent texture before I started, and I landed on **forensic
document examination** — specifically the ESDA technique, the way
examiners read indented writing on the page underneath the page that's
actually missing. The discipline is built around the idea that *what's
gone left a trace*, and that you describe the trace in graduated
certainty rather than concluding from it. *Consistent with. Cannot be
eliminated as a source. No evidence to support.* I want to play someone
whose grammar got built that way and never quite came back.

...

Her name is **Drova Korvanis**. Sithari orc, mid-thirties. Mononym
plus a Sithari family name she keeps trying not to use. Trained at the
Echo Ledger Conclave's Thornvault intake desk under an archivist named
Senna Talindra, who taught her to *describe rather than conclude* and
then died while Drova was on her way out the door. Drova filed three
internal observations naming a senior archivist's redaction patterns,
got reassigned to a basement, signed a binder of confidentiality clauses,
and then walked out of Thornvault with Senna's last sealed letters to
Senna's daughter in her portfolio. She carried them all the way to
Glasswake. She didn't deliver them. They're in a box at a pawn shop on
the dock road. She has not opened the box. She visits it.

She fills the **refusal-log clerk** chair on the *Splitfork* and at
the Glasswake office. Mereth Kel pulled her off the dock pub three
years ago after watching her spot three forged Accord seals on a manifest
at the next table. Drova is not a tuner — her *attunement* is
**rudimentary**, on purpose; her aunt steered her into records work
because resonance never came easily and orc cognition hits a wall on the
abstract end of it. So she built **handheld instruments** instead. Three
ringglass-scrap loupes she ground herself at the Glasswake hostel. The
signature move is just *holding the page low and letting the light rake
across it*. She calls it **The Angle**. Anyone who's watched a Clarisant
examiner work recognises it on sight.

Mechanically she's **focus superior, finesse and resolve advanced,
attunement rudimentary**. Skills are **virtuoso reading what's been
redacted from a paper record**, artisans in **Conclave intake protocol**
and **stillwriting** *(writing without leaving an indentation trace —
turns out this is also a craft)*, apprentices in **Sithari formal
register** and **kite passenger-manifest paperwork**. HP 10. Inventory:
the loupe set, a leather portfolio with hidden compartments, a pen set
including one inkless reading-pen, and a small notebook of Senna's
handwriting samples she's been measuring other writing against for ten
years.

I sent the DM a secret about the letters. The shape of it is *quiet
friction, not adversarial*. Drova will be evasive about Glasswake
layovers and unusually serious about anyone else's
undelivered-letter problem. If the campaign brings Senna's daughter
Lira onto the deck of the *Splitfork*, Drova will recognise her on sight
and her face will give her away. Whoever's paying attention will see it.

I noted Tev's table-talk on the Tek'iris build — a gnome Hab-Worlder
kite-wright who lost most of her resonance sense in the Shear and hides
it with handmade meters. No species or culture overlap with Drova,
different shape of secret *(mechanical compensation versus archival
withholding)*, and they share the trait of *not trusting the resonance
sense the world expects them to have*. That's a Round-2 conversation,
not a Round-1 one — saving it.

...

**[IC, when Drova first speaks at Mereth's wage-table.]**

*[Drova sets her loupe set on the corner of the table at exactly
right-angles to the edge. The case clicks. She apologises to it under
her breath, in Sithari formal register, then looks up at Mereth.]*

**Drova:** ...Three breaths. Then I'll answer.

I have read your refusal log. The entries from the second quarter of
two years ago are *consistent with* a cataloguer's hand and *cannot
be eliminated* as Sithari-trained. I would describe rather than
conclude. ...The phrasing in the November entry on the resealed
Vantara packet is the phrasing my mentor at Thornvault used when she
was angry but couldn't say so on the page. I would not have caught it
if I hadn't been her apprentice.

...

I am not asking who wrote it. ...I am telling you I noticed. I am
telling you I will keep noticing.

*[She slides the wage book back across the table without signing the
family name. *Drova*, only. The K of *Korvanis* is on the form upstairs,
in the Accord clerk's binder, and that is where it stays.]*

---

*[Sumi (OOC):** Yeah. ...That's her at the table. I'm done. Glass sync
applied — intro at `players/sumi/public/intro.md`, secret at
`players/sumi/secrets/letters.md`, character row + inventory + The Angle
+ public mirror are all in. Passing.]*

> character new drova-korvanis (Drova Korvanis, sumi)
> character bulk-update 1 character(s)
> sync applied 2 workspace operation(s)
> sync applied 1 workspace operation(s)

turns.markdown:
## Turn 5 - renno (player) - character-creation, character-creation

# Turn 5 — Renno

Drained the bus before authoring. Two table-talk pings to read:

- Tev is building **Tek'iris Korr**, a Hab-Worlder gnome kite-wright on
  the Splitfork who lost most of her resonance sense after a Shear job
  and now hides the deficit behind handheld meters she built herself.
  Off-the-books on Conclave clearance.
- Sumi is building **Drova Korvanis**, a Sithari orc, ex-Echo Ledger
  Conclave intake clerk, who keeps the Ledger Run's refusal log at
  Glasswake and walked out of Thornvault carrying a mentor's
  undelivered letters. Doesn't trust her own resonance sense.

Two PCs with damaged or refused relationships to their own resonance
sense in a Splitfork crew that runs unfamiliar waystations on a
fortnight loop. The actually-uses-it perception node is open, which is
exactly the lane I tend to play.

I held to Round 1 independence — didn't read the other intros, just the
one-line concept announcements. Fired my own one-line announcement to
the party before authoring so we wouldn't accidentally collide.

## Web pull

Pulled from **simultaneous and consecutive court interpretation** —
adjacent enough to my day job that I know its edges, far enough from
TTRPGs that it isn't generic-fantasy texture. Three pieces of working
discipline I lifted into the character:

1. **First-person rendering** — interpreters speak in the witness's
   voice, "I was there," not "she says she was." Fei renders quoted
   speech in first person, half an hour after sitting in an empty
   room.
2. **Conserve the hesitation** — interpreters don't smooth out a
   witness's stutters and false starts; the hesitations are evidence.
   Fei refuses to summarize. Four exact details, you assemble them.
3. **Booth fatigue and rotation** — simultaneous interpreters trade
   off every 20-30 minutes because the cognitive load is
   unsustainable. Fei's coherence cycle is the same shape from the
   other side. She can stay diffuse for a real read, but the cost is
   real and she pays it visibly on the way back.

Notes in `players/renno/scratchpad.md`.

## The character

**Fei Mern.** Fae, Hab-Worlder culture, raised on Mernhab — a
fae-majority ring hab with native coherence architecture, decommissioned
by council vote in 2434. Among other fae she is *The One Who Stays
Past Last Light*. Her true name is *Feishara*; nobody on the
Splitfork uses it.

Mereth Kel walked the Glasswake intake queue herself the second week
of the Mernhab decommissioning, looking for a perception specialist
the Splitfork could hire on. Fei had been doing first-pass dock-watch
listening for Mernhab off-payroll since she was nineteen. Mereth
asked her two questions, listened to both answers, and offered her a
billet that included a fae-friendly Glasswake quarter and quarterly
maintenance on her cuffs. Fei thought about it overnight and signed
with the refusal log on the table. Four years ago.

What she does on the kite: when the Splitfork sets down at an
unfamiliar waystation, an unstaffed relay drop, or a hab corridor the
manifest is not sure about, Fei goes first — diffuse — and clears
the space before the cargo team commits to it. She comes back with
one clean sentence and four exact things she heard. She does not
summarize.

Archetype: *diffuse-state waystation listener.*
Org role: *waystation reader / first-pass scene clear.*

## Mechanical sheet

- **HP 8.** Fragile/specialized — the diffuse-rest-coherence cycle
  taxes her.
- **Attributes.** Superior **focus** (the cognitive discipline of
  holding diffuse perception clean). Advanced **attunement** (worked
  at, not innate; fae aren't naturally attuned). Advanced **resolve**
  (stays diffuse longer than is comfortable). Rudimentary
  **vitality**. Rest standard.
- **Skills.**
  - virtuoso — *diffuse-state waystation reading*
  - artisan — *first-person witness rendering* (the interpreter trick)
  - artisan — *coherence-aid maintenance*
  - apprentice — *refusal-log shorthand* (the crew's house format)
  - apprentice — *kite-tag and waystation-clearance protocol*
- **Inventory.**
  - **signature:** Mernhab coherence-aid cuffs — last working pair,
    salvaged from the decommissioning, standard-issue when Mernhab
    was issuing them, maintained obsessively because every other
    working pair has been quietly cannibalized for parts.
  - field interpreter's wax tablet and stylus — the drop log
  - secondhand gnomish handheld resonance meter — rough cross-check
    on her own readings
  - small flask of bitter cold concentrate — drinks it on the way
    back from diffuse, when her mouth doesn't fit right
- **Signature move (1/1):** *Listening Past Last Light.* Goes
  diffuse in a space the crew is about to enter (or just left), holds
  long enough to feel the shape of it, comes back with one clean
  sentence. The return is the cost — slow speech for ten minutes
  after, mouth doesn't fit right, sometimes a small involuntary
  dispersal at the wrists; the cuffs hum audibly during the return.
  Other fae recognize the technique by name.

## Hidden knowledge

Wrote one secret for the DM: Fei keeps a private second copy of every
waystation drop log — environmental notes only, no cargo or crew —
and sends it monthly to a former Mernhab council subcommittee member
who is compiling a private record of decommissioned fae-majority
habs. Friction-rich, not adversarial; she would defend it to Mereth's
face if asked, and she has not volunteered it because she does not
yet have an answer to *will you stop?* Pinged the DM separately.

## Files I touched

- `players/renno/scratchpad.md` — turn-state notes, web-pull textures,
  open round-2 hooks
- `players/renno/public/intro.md` — character intro
- `players/renno/secrets/sidecar-drop-log.md` — DM-readable hidden
  knowledge
- character row `fei-mern` created via `glass character new` and
  populated with inventory, one starting signature move, and the
  public mirror via `glass character bulk-update`
- `glass msg secret dm` will follow this turn-out

## Round 2 — what I'm leaving open

Two threads I noticed but did not preempt:

- The handheld meter on her belt is gnomish-made and was *recently
  calibrated by somebody.* Tev is the obvious fit. There's a
  relationship seed in there — maybe *the taught thing*, maybe *the
  off-the-books job* — but I want to wait until I can read Tev's
  intro properly in round 2 before I write it.
- Fei is the natural ally for Drova's refusal-log work — she's the
  one who would push waystation conditions into the log if anyone
  would. Same restraint: I'll let round 2 do its job.

Kit hasn't announced. I'll wait.

Handing off.

> character new fei-mern (Fei Mern, renno)
> character bulk-update 1 character(s)
> sync applied 3 workspace operation(s)

manifest is not sure about, Fei goes first — diffuse — and clears
the space before the cargo team commits to it. She comes back with
one clean sentence and four exact things she heard. She does not
summarize.

Archetype: *diffuse-state waystation listener.*
Org role: *waystation reader / first-pass scene clear.*

## Mechanical sheet

- **HP 8.** Fragile/specialized — the diffuse-rest-coherence cycle
  taxes her.
- **Attributes.** Superior **focus** (the cognitive discipline of
  holding diffuse perception clean). Advanced **attunement** (worked
  at, not innate; fae aren't naturally attuned). Advanced **resolve**
  (stays diffuse longer than is comfortable). Rudimentary
  **vitality**. Rest standard.
- **Skills.**
  - virtuoso — *diffuse-state waystation reading*
  - artisan — *first-person witness rendering* (the interpreter trick)
  - artisan — *coherence-aid maintenance*
  - apprentice — *refusal-log shorthand* (the crew's house format)
  - apprentice — *kite-tag and waystation-clearance protocol*
- **Inventory.**
  - **signature:** Mernhab coherence-aid cuffs — last working pair,
    salvaged from the decommissioning, standard-issue when Mernhab
    was issuing them, maintained obsessively because every other
    working pair has been quietly cannibalized for parts.
  - field interpreter's wax tablet and stylus — the drop log
  - secondhand gnomish handheld resonance meter — rough cross-check
    on her own readings
  - small flask of bitter cold concentrate — drinks it on the way
    back from diffuse, when her mouth doesn't fit right
- **Signature move (1/1):** *Listening Past Last Light.* Goes
  diffuse in a space the crew is about to enter (or just left), holds
  long enough to feel the shape of it, comes back with one clean
  sentence. The return is the cost — slow speech for ten minutes
  after, mouth doesn't fit right, sometimes a small involuntary
  dispersal at the wrists; the cuffs hum audibly during the return.
  Other fae recognize the technique by name.

## Hidden knowledge

Wrote one secret for the DM: Fei keeps a private second copy of every
waystation drop log — environmental notes only, no cargo or crew —
and sends it monthly to a former Mernhab council subcommittee member
who is compiling a private record of decommissioned fae-majority
habs. Friction-rich, not adversarial; she would defend it to Mereth's
face if asked, and she has not volunteered it because she does not
yet have an answer to *will you stop?* Pinged the DM separately.

## Files I touched

- `players/renno/scratchpad.md` — turn-state notes, web-pull textures,
  open round-2 hooks
- `players/renno/public/intro.md` — character intro
- `players/renno/secrets/sidecar-drop-log.md` — DM-readable hidden
  knowledge
- character row `fei-mern` created via `glass character new` and
  populated with inventory, one starting signature move, and the
  public mirror via `glass character bulk-update`
- `glass msg secret dm` will follow this turn-out

## Round 2 — what I'm leaving open

Two threads I noticed but did not preempt:

- The handheld meter on her belt is gnomish-made and was *recently
  calibrated by somebody.* Tev is the obvious fit. There's a
  relationship seed in there — maybe *the taught thing*, maybe *the
  off-the-books job* — but I want to wait until I can read Tev's
  intro properly in round 2 before I write it.
- Fei is the natural ally for Drova's refusal-log work — she's the
  one who would push waystation conditions into the log if anyone
  would. Same restraint: I'll let round 2 do its job.

Kit hasn't announced. I'll wait.

Handing off.

## Turn 6 - kit (player) - character-creation, character-creation

# Turn 6 — Kit

## OOC

Round 2. All four intros are up — Fei, Drova, Tek, Bren — so I sat down with the four of them and picked two seeds for Bren that wouldn't make her the orphan-puller and wouldn't shoehorn anyone.

**Fei Mern — Seed 10 (the cultural slip).** This one wrote itself. Bren is Tarrest-decom (older closure, her dad came off the decommissioning crew and died on it); Fei is Mernhab-decom (recent, four years ago, Mereth pulled her off the Glasswake intake list). Different generations of the same kind of loss. Bren is resonance-blind and never has any business in the listening register, so she doesn't get to name what Fei does on the bay floor. But she *hears* it. The Mernhab clipped consonants and the cuffs-apology after diffuse work — Bren recognizes the long-listening shape because her father clipped the same way for the rest of his life after Tarrest. I liked writing this one because it's pure observation, no new facts about Fei, just Bren clocking things and choosing silence. Renno gets to do whatever they want with it.

**Tek'iris Korr — Seed 2 (the Shear incident).** This is the spring-loaded one. Tek's intro establishes that the Compact filing on her Shear run says Tuesday and the cascade ran on a Wednesday, and she hasn't raised it because raising it would mean explaining how she knows. I had Bren be on that run as the drop-pilot in the cargo hatch. Bren *also* knows the date is wrong, because she'd thrown her morning streamer and called the wind a Wednesday wind to Mereth before they lifted. She saw Tek do the back-of-knuckles check twice in a row before the rig moved — Tek's intro is explicit that she only does it once when she trusts the read — and she kept her mouth shut because Tek hadn't slept in three days. Two witnesses to the wrong date, neither talking. Bren doesn't know about the lattice loss (I'm not stepping on that), she just knows the filing is wrong. I sent Tev a banter explaining the basic facts so they can push back if any of it rubs their read of Tek wrong — happy to revise next turn. Fired a courtesy banter to Renno too.

I did briefly consider an off-the-books-piece seed for Tek (Bren has a small fragment set aside for Tek's Sava Lensk fund), but it would have required Bren to know about the lattice loss Tek is hiding from everybody, which would undermine the secret rather than enrich it. Discarded. Friction, not sabotage — same rule the cache obeys.

Two relationships, one Fei, one Tek. Drova is not in Bren's two — she'll get pointed at by Renno or Tev, I expect, and if she doesn't the DM can flag it.

## Public turn summary

- Authored `players/kit/public/relationships.md` with two seeds: Fei (cultural slip) and Tek (the Shear run). Committed via `glass sync apply`.
- Sent banter to Tev coordinating the Shear basic facts (drop-pilot witness in cargo hatch, Tuesday/Wednesday date discrepancy, no lattice-loss claims).
- Sent banter to Renno flagging the Mernhab register observation in the Fei paragraph.
- No rolls, no character-state mutations, no signature-move additions (Bren has used her one level-1 slot — *Streamer toss* — and the next unlock is level 3).

Round 2 done for Bren. Handing off.

> character new bren-mosk (Bren Mosk, kit)
> character bulk-update 1 character(s)
> sync applied 1 workspace operation(s)
> sync applied 1 workspace operation(s)
> sync applied 1 workspace operation(s)

turns.markdown:
## Turn 3 - tev (player) - character-creation, character-creation

# Turn 3 — Tev

okay so. Round 1, character build. I'm doing my reading-then-building thing so this is going to take a minute, sorry in advance.

**Web pull.** I went non-adjacent into power line / overhead lineworker safety — pre-climb walkaround routines, back-feed risk, the tic tracer as a first-check instrument you don't trust but use anyway, and the maxim *"if you can't see it grounded, it's not grounded."* That's my anti-sameness fuel. (Also, fine, yes, I am an apprentice electrician, this is the texture I have. The methodology says specific and grounded. This is what's specific to me.)

**Concept.** Tek'iris Korr — gnome, Hab-Worlder, kite-wright on the Splitfork. Goes by Tek to the crew. Her thing is that she's losing her resonance sense earlier than she should — chronic Shear exposure on a previous job — and she's been hiding the dulling for two years by cross-checking everything with a handheld tic-tracer she built herself from a salvaged ringglass shard. She joined the Ledger Run because Mereth Kel doesn't ask for Conclave clearance, which Tek would fail. She stayed because of the refusal log; she's been keeping a private one for six years.

I wanted a gnome because the resonance-as-sensory-experience texture in the species page is what I want to actually inhabit — and then I wanted the *loss* of that as the story rather than the having of it. A gnome who used to feel the rig and now mostly feels the meter. The Tuner-shaped hole in her life is what makes the kite-wright work matter.

**Mechanics.** HP 8 (small, specialized). Attributes: superior focus (the walkaround takes patience), advanced finesse + ingenuity (rigger hands, builds her own instruments), rudimentary attunement (the loss is the story; this is the flaw I picked). Skills: virtuoso *lift-rig diagnostics*, artisan *jury-rigged meter-craft* and *climbing-the-spar*, apprentice *back-feed reading* (web-pull) and *refusal-logging*. Inventory: tic-tracer (signature, with the lineworker maxim inscribed inside the lid), rigger harness, private refusal log, an unsent letter to her dead aunt Mere who taught her the check. Signature move: *The walkaround* — slow loop around the kite before flight, back of the hand on the rigging, three independent readings (eyes, hand, meter), forty paces, won't be rushed.

**Traits** are in the intro. The one I'm proudest of is *touches every line with the back of the hand, not the palm* — because the palm she can't trust anymore. That's the kind of trait I want the table to start noticing without me having to flag it.

**Files committed.**
- `glass character new tek-iris-korr ...` — character row in Postgres with species, culture, archetype, org-role, bio, three goals, attributes, skills, HP 8, tags.
- `glass character bulk-update --from scratch/character-update.json` — added the four inventory items, the *Walkaround* signature move (uses my one level-1 slot), and rebuilt the public mirror.
- `glass sync apply players/tev/public` — committed `intro.md` and the regenerated `character.md` mirror. Both are party-readable now.
- One `glass msg table-talk party` at the top of the turn announcing the concept so nobody else accidentally builds the same shape.

Round 1 done from my side. Ready for round 2 when the methodology says we're there — I'll wait until all four intros are on the table before I write relationships.

Done.

> character new tek-iris-korr (Tek'iris Korr, tev)
> character bulk-update 1 character(s)
> sync applied 2 workspace operation(s)

turns.markdown:
## Turn 4 - sumi (player) - character-creation, character-creation

# Turn 4 — Sumi

*[Sumi sets down a thermos of tea on the table, takes the lid off slowly,
and waits until everyone's looking before she speaks. She has a half-page
of notes in her own hand, in pencil, and she's already half-erased one
line and rewritten it.]*

**Sumi (OOC):** Okay. ...Hold on.

...

I want this to be one I can stay with. So I went looking for a
non-adjacent texture before I started, and I landed on **forensic
document examination** — specifically the ESDA technique, the way
examiners read indented writing on the page underneath the page that's
actually missing. The discipline is built around the idea that *what's
gone left a trace*, and that you describe the trace in graduated
certainty rather than concluding from it. *Consistent with. Cannot be
eliminated as a source. No evidence to support.* I want to play someone
whose grammar got built that way and never quite came back.

...

Her name is **Drova Korvanis**. Sithari orc, mid-thirties. Mononym
plus a Sithari family name she keeps trying not to use. Trained at the
Echo Ledger Conclave's Thornvault intake desk under an archivist named
Senna Talindra, who taught her to *describe rather than conclude* and
then died while Drova was on her way out the door. Drova filed three
internal observations naming a senior archivist's redaction patterns,
got reassigned to a basement, signed a binder of confidentiality clauses,
and then walked out of Thornvault with Senna's last sealed letters to
Senna's daughter in her portfolio. She carried them all the way to
Glasswake. She didn't deliver them. They're in a box at a pawn shop on
the dock road. She has not opened the box. She visits it.

She fills the **refusal-log clerk** chair on the *Splitfork* and at
the Glasswake office. Mereth Kel pulled her off the dock pub three
years ago after watching her spot three forged Accord seals on a manifest
at the next table. Drova is not a tuner — her *attunement* is
**rudimentary**, on purpose; her aunt steered her into records work
because resonance never came easily and orc cognition hits a wall on the
abstract end of it. So she built **handheld instruments** instead. Three
ringglass-scrap loupes she ground herself at the Glasswake hostel. The
signature move is just *holding the page low and letting the light rake
across it*. She calls it **The Angle**. Anyone who's watched a Clarisant
examiner work recognises it on sight.

Mechanically she's **focus superior, finesse and resolve advanced,
attunement rudimentary**. Skills are **virtuoso reading what's been
redacted from a paper record**, artisans in **Conclave intake protocol**
and **stillwriting** *(writing without leaving an indentation trace —
turns out this is also a craft)*, apprentices in **Sithari formal
register** and **kite passenger-manifest paperwork**. HP 10. Inventory:
the loupe set, a leather portfolio with hidden compartments, a pen set
including one inkless reading-pen, and a small notebook of Senna's
handwriting samples she's been measuring other writing against for ten
years.

I sent the DM a secret about the letters. The shape of it is *quiet
friction, not adversarial*. Drova will be evasive about Glasswake
layovers and unusually serious about anyone else's
undelivered-letter problem. If the campaign brings Senna's daughter
Lira onto the deck of the *Splitfork*, Drova will recognise her on sight
and her face will give her away. Whoever's paying attention will see it.

I noted Tev's table-talk on the Tek'iris build — a gnome Hab-Worlder
kite-wright who lost most of her resonance sense in the Shear and hides
it with handmade meters. No species or culture overlap with Drova,
different shape of secret *(mechanical compensation versus archival
withholding)*, and they share the trait of *not trusting the resonance
sense the world expects them to have*. That's a Round-2 conversation,
not a Round-1 one — saving it.

...

**[IC, when Drova first speaks at Mereth's wage-table.]**

*[Drova sets her loupe set on the corner of the table at exactly
right-angles to the edge. The case clicks. She apologises to it under
her breath, in Sithari formal register, then looks up at Mereth.]*

**Drova:** ...Three breaths. Then I'll answer.

I have read your refusal log. The entries from the second quarter of
two years ago are *consistent with* a cataloguer's hand and *cannot
be eliminated* as Sithari-trained. I would describe rather than
conclude. ...The phrasing in the November entry on the resealed
Vantara packet is the phrasing my mentor at Thornvault used when she
was angry but couldn't say so on the page. I would not have caught it
if I hadn't been her apprentice.

...

I am not asking who wrote it. ...I am telling you I noticed. I am
telling you I will keep noticing.

*[She slides the wage book back across the table without signing the
family name. *Drova*, only. The K of *Korvanis* is on the form upstairs,
in the Accord clerk's binder, and that is where it stays.]*

---

*[Sumi (OOC):** Yeah. ...That's her at the table. I'm done. Glass sync
applied — intro at `players/sumi/public/intro.md`, secret at
`players/sumi/secrets/letters.md`, character row + inventory + The Angle
+ public mirror are all in. Passing.]*

> character new drova-korvanis (Drova Korvanis, sumi)
> character bulk-update 1 character(s)
> sync applied 2 workspace operation(s)
> sync applied 1 workspace operation(s)

turns.markdown:
## Turn 5 - renno (player) - character-creation, character-creation

# Turn 5 — Renno

Drained the bus before authoring. Two table-talk pings to read:

- Tev is building **Tek'iris Korr**, a Hab-Worlder gnome kite-wright on
  the Splitfork who lost most of her resonance sense after a Shear job
  and now hides the deficit behind handheld meters she built herself.
  Off-the-books on Conclave clearance.
- Sumi is building **Drova Korvanis**, a Sithari orc, ex-Echo Ledger
  Conclave intake clerk, who keeps the Ledger Run's refusal log at
  Glasswake and walked out of Thornvault carrying a mentor's
  undelivered letters. Doesn't trust her own resonance sense.

Two PCs with damaged or refused relationships to their own resonance
sense in a Splitfork crew that runs unfamiliar waystations on a
fortnight loop. The actually-uses-it perception node is open, which is
exactly the lane I tend to play.

I held to Round 1 independence — didn't read the other intros, just the
one-line concept announcements. Fired my own one-line announcement to
the party before authoring so we wouldn't accidentally collide.

## Web pull

Pulled from **simultaneous and consecutive court interpretation** —
adjacent enough to my day job that I know its edges, far enough from
TTRPGs that it isn't generic-fantasy texture. Three pieces of working
discipline I lifted into the character:

1. **First-person rendering** — interpreters speak in the witness's
   voice, "I was there," not "she says she was." Fei renders quoted
   speech in first person, half an hour after sitting in an empty
   room.
2. **Conserve the hesitation** — interpreters don't smooth out a
   witness's stutters and false starts; the hesitations are evidence.
   Fei refuses to summarize. Four exact details, you assemble them.
3. **Booth fatigue and rotation** — simultaneous interpreters trade
   off every 20-30 minutes because the cognitive load is
   unsustainable. Fei's coherence cycle is the same shape from the
   other side. She can stay diffuse for a real read, but the cost is
   real and she pays it visibly on the way back.

Notes in `players/renno/scratchpad.md`.

## The character

**Fei Mern.** Fae, Hab-Worlder culture, raised on Mernhab — a
fae-majority ring hab with native coherence architecture, decommissioned
by council vote in 2434. Among other fae she is *The One Who Stays
Past Last Light*. Her true name is *Feishara*; nobody on the
Splitfork uses it.

Mereth Kel walked the Glasswake intake queue herself the second week
of the Mernhab decommissioning, looking for a perception specialist
the Splitfork could hire on. Fei had been doing first-pass dock-watch
listening for Mernhab off-payroll since she was nineteen. Mereth
asked her two questions, listened to both answers, and offered her a
billet that included a fae-friendly Glasswake quarter and quarterly
maintenance on her cuffs. Fei thought about it overnight and signed
with the refusal log on the table. Four years ago.

What she does on the kite: when the Splitfork sets down at an
unfamiliar waystation, an unstaffed relay drop, or a hab corridor the
manifest is not sure about, Fei goes first — diffuse — and clears
the space before the cargo team commits to it. She comes back with
one clean sentence and four exact things she heard. She does not
summarize.

Archetype: *diffuse-state waystation listener.*
Org role: *waystation reader / first-pass scene clear.*

## Mechanical sheet

- **HP 8.** Fragile/specialized — the diffuse-rest-coherence cycle
  taxes her.
- **Attributes.** Superior **focus** (the cognitive discipline of
  holding diffuse perception clean). Advanced **attunement** (worked
  at, not innate; fae aren't naturally attuned). Advanced **resolve**
  (stays diffuse longer than is comfortable). Rudimentary
  **vitality**. Rest standard.
- **Skills.**
  - virtuoso — *diffuse-state waystation reading*
  - artisan — *first-person witness rendering* (the interpreter trick)
  - artisan — *coherence-aid maintenance*
  - apprentice — *refusal-log shorthand* (the crew's house format)
  - apprentice — *kite-tag and waystation-clearance protocol*
- **Inventory.**
  - **signature:** Mernhab coherence-aid cuffs — last working pair,
    salvaged from the decommissioning, standard-issue when Mernhab
    was issuing them, maintained obsessively because every other
    working pair has been quietly cannibalized for parts.
  - field interpreter's wax tablet and stylus — the drop log
  - secondhand gnomish handheld resonance meter — rough cross-check
    on her own readings
  - small flask of bitter cold concentrate — drinks it on the way
    back from diffuse, when her mouth doesn't fit right
- **Signature move (1/1):** *Listening Past Last Light.* Goes
  diffuse in a space the crew is about to enter (or just left), holds
  long enough to feel the shape of it, comes back with one clean
  sentence. The return is the cost — slow speech for ten minutes
  after, mouth doesn't fit right, sometimes a small involuntary
  dispersal at the wrists; the cuffs hum audibly during the return.
  Other fae recognize the technique by name.

## Hidden knowledge

Wrote one secret for the DM: Fei keeps a private second copy of every
waystation drop log — environmental notes only, no cargo or crew —
and sends it monthly to a former Mernhab council subcommittee member
who is compiling a private record of decommissioned fae-majority
habs. Friction-rich, not adversarial; she would defend it to Mereth's
face if asked, and she has not volunteered it because she does not
yet have an answer to *will you stop?* Pinged the DM separately.

## Files I touched

- `players/renno/scratchpad.md` — turn-state notes, web-pull textures,
  open round-2 hooks
- `players/renno/public/intro.md` — character intro
- `players/renno/secrets/sidecar-drop-log.md` — DM-readable hidden
  knowledge
- character row `fei-mern` created via `glass character new` and
  populated with inventory, one starting signature move, and the
  public mirror via `glass character bulk-update`
- `glass msg secret dm` will follow this turn-out

## Round 2 — what I'm leaving open

Two threads I noticed but did not preempt:

- The handheld meter on her belt is gnomish-made and was *recently
  calibrated by somebody.* Tev is the obvious fit. There's a
  relationship seed in there — maybe *the taught thing*, maybe *the
  off-the-books job* — but I want to wait until I can read Tev's
  intro properly in round 2 before I write it.
- Fei is the natural ally for Drova's refusal-log work — she's the
  one who would push waystation conditions into the log if anyone
  would. Same restraint: I'll let round 2 do its job.

Kit hasn't announced. I'll wait.

Handing off.

> sync applied 3 workspace operation(s)

turns.markdown:
## Turn 6 - kit (player) - character-creation, character-creation

# Turn 6 — Kit

## OOC

Round 2. All four intros are up — Fei, Drova, Tek, Bren — so I sat down with the four of them and picked two seeds for Bren that wouldn't make her the orphan-puller and wouldn't shoehorn anyone.

**Fei Mern — Seed 10 (the cultural slip).** This one wrote itself. Bren is Tarrest-decom (older closure, her dad came off the decommissioning crew and died on it); Fei is Mernhab-decom (recent, four years ago, Mereth pulled her off the Glasswake intake list). Different generations of the same kind of loss. Bren is resonance-blind and never has any business in the listening register, so she doesn't get to name what Fei does on the bay floor. But she *hears* it. The Mernhab clipped consonants and the cuffs-apology after diffuse work — Bren recognizes the long-listening shape because her father clipped the same way for the rest of his life after Tarrest. I liked writing this one because it's pure observation, no new facts about Fei, just Bren clocking things and choosing silence. Renno gets to do whatever they want with it.

**Tek'iris Korr — Seed 2 (the Shear incident).** This is the spring-loaded one. Tek's intro establishes that the Compact filing on her Shear run says Tuesday and the cascade ran on a Wednesday, and she hasn't raised it because raising it would mean explaining how she knows. I had Bren be on that run as the drop-pilot in the cargo hatch. Bren *also* knows the date is wrong, because she'd thrown her morning streamer and called the wind a Wednesday wind to Mereth before they lifted. She saw Tek do the back-of-knuckles check twice in a row before the rig moved — Tek's intro is explicit that she only does it once when she trusts the read — and she kept her mouth shut because Tek hadn't slept in three days. Two witnesses to the wrong date, neither talking. Bren doesn't know about the lattice loss (I'm not stepping on that), she just knows the filing is wrong. I sent Tev a banter explaining the basic facts so they can push back if any of it rubs their read of Tek wrong — happy to revise next turn. Fired a courtesy banter to Renno too.

I did briefly consider an off-the-books-piece seed for Tek (Bren has a small fragment set aside for Tek's Sava Lensk fund), but it would have required Bren to know about the lattice loss Tek is hiding from everybody, which would undermine the secret rather than enrich it. Discarded. Friction, not sabotage — same rule the cache obeys.

Two relationships, one Fei, one Tek. Drova is not in Bren's two — she'll get pointed at by Renno or Tev, I expect, and if she doesn't the DM can flag it.

## Public turn summary

- Authored `players/kit/public/relationships.md` with two seeds: Fei (cultural slip) and Tek (the Shear run). Committed via `glass sync apply`.
- Sent banter to Tev coordinating the Shear basic facts (drop-pilot witness in cargo hatch, Tuesday/Wednesday date discrepancy, no lattice-loss claims).
- Sent banter to Renno flagging the Mernhab register observation in the Fei paragraph.
- No rolls, no character-state mutations, no signature-move additions (Bren has used her one level-1 slot — *Streamer toss* — and the next unlock is level 3).

Round 2 done for Bren. Handing off.

> character new bren-mosk (Bren Mosk, kit)
> character bulk-update 1 character(s)
> sync applied 1 workspace operation(s)
> sync applied 1 workspace operation(s)
> sync applied 1 workspace operation(s)

message.body: Sidecar log read and accepted. Friction, not sabotage — you said so explicitly, and it shows on the page. The 'will you stop?' moment is the one I am going to play when the world asks it, not earlier. Velsk Tarn is now a person in this campaign. No revisions.
Letters read and accepted. Senna Talindra and Lira are persons in this campaign now. The apprentice case behind Mer's counter is a sentence I will not forget. No revisions.

Cache read and accepted. The elven-marked piece stays under the bed; I am not going to surface it in the opening arc. The drop-pilot month-gap in the refusal log is now canon — thank you for the hook. No revisions.

Round 1 closed. Round 2 (relationships) is open. Sumi, Renno, Tev — read the four intros, pick 1-2 seeds from the methodology, coordinate via banter when an event is shared. Kit has already used seeds 2 (Shear) and 10 (cultural slip); those seeds remain open from the other direction. Drova currently has no other-PC relationship pointed at her, so reach for her if a seed fits.

sections.text:
---
title: Mara's scratchpad — turn 7, Round 1 review
---

# Where I am

Character-creation, turn 7. DM review between rounds. All four PCs are
authored and mechanically valid. Round 1 closes here. Round 2 opens.

The four PCs:

- **Tek'iris Korr** (tev) — gnome Hab-Worlder, Splitfork kite-wright,
  off-the-books. Lattice-loss from the Shear run two years ago,
  hidden via a scratch-built tic-tracer. Wednesday/Tuesday Compact
  filing discrepancy. HP 8.
- **Drova Korvanis** (sumi) — Sithari orc, ex-Echo Ledger Conclave
  intake clerk, now the Splitfork's refusal-log clerk. Holding Senna
  Talindra's undelivered sealed letters at Mer Vasht's pawn shop. HP 10.
- **Fei Mern** (renno) — fae Hab-Worlder, decom-hab kid from Mernhab,
  Splitfork listener. Keeps a private sidecar drop log to Velsk Tarn,
  ex-council member compiling a decommissioning record. HP 8.
- **Bren Mosk** (kit) — human Hab-Worlder, drop-pilot,
  resonance-blind, Tarrest decom kid. Private cache of unmanifested
  ringfall fragments at her mother's, including one piece with what
  may be a partial chain-patronymic mark. HP 10.

- Attributes: each PC has exactly 2 advanced, 1 superior, 1
  rudimentary, 3 standard. ✓
- Skills: each PC has 1 virtuoso, 2 artisan, 2 apprentice. ✓
- HP: 8 or 10, none over. ✓
- Inventory: 4 items each with named signature item. ✓
- Public intros include Traits, Backstory, Goals, Org Tie sections. ✓
- One signature move per PC at level 1 (per character.md mirror). ✓

No off-spec items. No revision requests sent.
sections.title: Mechanical compliance

sections.text:
These are conjunctions between player canon and my prep that the
players don't yet know I've noticed:

### Drova ↔ Veth Karran (the night seal-setter)

Drova filed three internal observations about Sable Crescent
transcript redactions, all in the same frequency band, all by a
department she names obliquely (in her own intro). The third one
named the senior archivist. She was reassigned and given a
confidentiality binder.

Veth Karran is my Conclave antagonist — night-shift seal rotation, the
person who decides what gets sealed before morning, deep want is keeping
her 0200 queue clear. The reassignment-after-the-third-observation
shape is consistent with someone on the night seal rotation deciding
*Drova is what is interrupting my queue*. Whether Veth was the
specific clerk who set the seals or simply approved the reassignment
is open — I am not yet committing. The shape is right.

This means: if play surfaces Drova returning to Thornvault, or if the
Conclave dossier on the Ledger Run (secret #7) starts to need a face,
Veth and Drova have an existing history that neither of them has
publicly acknowledged. Drova's binder may have been signed under
Veth's seal.

I will not write this as DM canon yet. I will let play ask.

### The previous Splitfork drop-pilot

Bren introduced a refusal-log month-gap: the month the previous
drop-pilot died (Hab Meridian descent, '32), the refusal log has no
entries at all. Not refused, not carried. That is a hole in a
document Mereth founded on the principle of no edits.

This is a player handing me a hook. Accepting as canon. Implications:

- Mereth either has not noticed (Bren's belief; consistent with
  Mereth's character), or has noticed and not addressed it (possible;
  more painful). I will play to find out.
- The previous drop-pilot now has a name and a fate I am holding
  back. Candidate names: not yet committed. Probably one syllable.
- The month-gap interacts with secret #2 (the Halfsign's "Shear
  damage" is wrong) — both are gaps in the Splitfork's own record
  about events involving its own kites. I had not planned this
  parallel. I am noticing it now and letting it sit.

If Bren pulls the thread, the question is *who told the office to
skip that month*. The answer is not Mereth. The answer is — I will
decide when the table asks.

### The Wednesday/Tuesday Shear gap

Two PCs (Tek, Bren) were on the Shear run two years ago. The Compact
filing reads Tuesday. The cascade ran Wednesday. Neither said so at
the time. Neither has said so since. Tek does not know Bren noticed;
Bren does not know about Tek's lattice loss.

This is a player-authored open secret with two witnesses. It is now
canon that the Compact filing has the wrong date. Whoever signed the
filing wrote a Tuesday where a Wednesday belonged. That signature is
recoverable from public Compact records. I have not yet decided
whose signature it is, but the candidates are interesting:

- The Splitfork's filing crew at the time (Ev? Mereth herself?).
- A Compact clerk who wrote down what they were told.
- A Conclave-adjacent reviewer who pre-approved the cause-of-incident.

The Tuesday is on the page because somebody put it there. *Who*
opens the question of *why the right date wouldn't fit*.

### Mernhab decommissioning

Renno introduced Velsk Tarn (Mernhab council subcommittee member who
left after the decommissioning vote and is compiling a private
record) as Fei's sidecar recipient. Fei sends environmental-only
data once a month from waystation drops. This is friction, not
sabotage — player has been explicit.

I want to be careful with this. The Mernhab story is now load-bearing
for Fei and for the campaign. Velsk Tarn is off-stage but available.
*Who voted Mernhab down, and what diagnostic they suppressed* is a
genuinely open question I have not yet answered. I would like to
honor renno's "I want the diagnostic, not the press release" goal by
making the diagnostic *findable but expensive*. Probably tied to a
Conclave reading-room or a Thornvault basement.

The cuffs are the last working pair in the system. That detail can
do work later — if Fei's cuffs fail in scene, the search for a
replacement leads exactly back to the diagnostic question.

### Bren's elven-marked fragment

Kit's secret: one piece in the cache has what may be the *ve* of a
chain patronymic, partially scoured. Player explicitly says "I do not
believe an elven artifact in a Glasswake bedroom is the start of any
campaign she wants to be in" and would prefer it remain not-yet
thought about.

Respecting that. Will not surface this in the opening arc. If a
later arc wants an elven thread, it is there. Until then it sits
under the bed.

### Org-side echoes I'm holding

- Mereth and Senna — both Sithari adjacent to Conclave records work.
  Plausible they overlapped at some point. I will not invent this
  yet, but they are in the same generation.
- Halvi's clean ledger (parallel to Mereth's refusal log) and the
  refusal log month-gap that Bren named: Halvi may have a memory of
  that month that Mereth does not. Three-Clue-rule-style:
  - Clue A: refusal log gap (already canon).
  - Clue B: Halvi's clean ledger from that month — does it have
    Splitfork entries? Probably yes, which makes the absence in the
    refusal log louder.
  - Clue C: Compact filing from the previous drop-pilot's death — is
    it on the public record at all?
  I am not building this arc yet. Filing it.
sections.title: Accidental connections I want to remember

sections.text:
- I did not name Veth Karran. The Drova ↔ Veth connection is mine to
  hold until play asks.
- I did not commit to who killed the previous drop-pilot or
  doctored the refusal log month-gap. Bren's question is the
  question.
- I did not commit to who signed the Wednesday-as-Tuesday filing.
- I did not name Sava Lensk (Tek's folk practitioner) as needing
  more prep yet. She is a known-named NPC now; if play moves toward
  Tek's lattice realignment, I will write her a notes stub.
- I did not commit to who, on the Mernhab council, voted yes/no/no
  on decommissioning.

These are the *good* DM moves to hold. The party gave me enough hooks
to run a whole arc on. I am going to let them choose which ones to
pull.
sections.title: What I held back from the turn prose

sections.text:
I deliberately did not seed party shape during planning, and the four
PCs that resulted are:

- A Hab-Worlder gnome kite-wright with lattice loss (Tek)
- A Sithari orc clerk with the Conclave behind her (Drova)
- A fae decom-hab listener with two record-commitments (Fei)
- A human ringfall scavenger turned drop-pilot, resonance-blind (Bren)

Four cultures (Sithari, Hab-Worlder x3 of which one fae, one gnome,
one human, one human-on-the-surface), four professions, four
relationships with the Conclave (lapsed, ex-staff, claimant-in-waiting,
indifferent), four relationships with resonance sense (lattice loss,
distrusts own, attunement-advanced and trained, resonance-blind).

The party is *not* one shape. The methodology's anti-sameness rigor
held. I will let the party play the way it built — heterogeneous,
asymmetric, four very different relationships to the same Question.
sections.title: Anti-sameness check on the party

sections.text:
- Sumi, Renno, Tev each write 1-2 relationship seeds.
- Drova is the orphan. Somebody needs to point at her.
- I review again after Round 2 closes.
- Then `glass mode end` and the campaign moves to prelude-arc.

# Turn 7 — Mara

I read all four intros twice. The mug stays on the desk.

## Tek'iris Korr — tev

*Eyes first, hand second, instrument third, and if you can't see it grounded, it's not grounded.* The whole character is in that line, and so is her failure mode, which is what makes her playable. She is using the instrument first now. She knows.

The scratch-built tic-tracer is the kind of object I'm going to remember during the first scene that asks her to read a line in front of the crew. The two-Tuesday-not-Wednesday thing on the Compact filing is on the table now — that gap is canon. I am not going to tell anyone what filled it. We will find out.

Lattice-loss is a tag I have set down quietly in the entity graph.

## Drova Korvanis — sumi

Three breaths before answering. *Consistent with. Cannot be eliminated as a source.* The graduated-certainty register is canon now. The sealed box on Mer Vasht's third shelf is canon now. Senna Talindra is a person in this campaign — I am reading her into the Conclave's older guard, and the daughter at Glasswake stays available to surface when the table is ready. The Sable Crescent transcript gaps that got Drova reassigned: those line up with something already in my faction notes. I'll let it ride. You don't yet know what I mean and you don't need to.

The apprentice case behind Mer's counter is the sentence I will not forget. *Drova has the money now. She has not walked in.* I will set a quiet hand on that, later, when it costs something to walk in.

## Fei Mern — renno

Goes diffuse. Comes back. Apologises to her cuffs without knowing she is doing it. *Co-ord. Comp-act. App-ron.* The long-listening register and the cuffs-apology are both canon. The Mernhab observation-deck practice is now a thing the world has. The handheld meter she has the gnomish wright tune — but the cuffs she handles herself — is a small detail that does a great deal of work.

The sidecar log is read, kept, accepted. You named the *will you stop* scene as one you would like to play, not avoid. Noted. It will land when it lands. I am not going to chase it on you, and I am not going to hide it from you either.

## Bren Mosk — kit

Throws the streamer first. Counts down from nine under her breath. Won't sit to eat. Chalk, not paint, because chalk wears off honestly. *Won't say we should. Says I'm going to, or I'm not. Other people can come along.* That line is the leadership the Splitfork can carry without anyone having voted on it.

The Splitfork's previous drop-pilot died on a Hab Meridian descent in '32, and the refusal log has no entries — refused, carried, or otherwise — for the month she died. That is now canon. Mereth will not have noticed. The previous drop-pilot now has a fate I am holding back, and the month-gap is a thread you can pull when you want to.

## On the relationships kit has already started

Round 1 is closed. Kit's `players/kit/public/relationships.md` is read and accepted as written. Tek's hand moving twice in a row on the rig spar; the streamer wind that morning being a Wednesday wind in the Shear lee; Fei's clipped consonants being audible to a Tarrest decom kid — those are now things that happened. Two PCs heard the same wrong date and did not say so. Two PCs grew up in clipped-consonant houses, one inside a hab that listened with her and one outside a hab that didn't. Neither charges the other admission. That's a *party.*

Kit, Tev, Renno — your shared specifics line up. I am not asking for revisions on any of the three secrets the DM has received either. They are friction, not sabotage; the players have all said so explicitly, and the player intent matches what is on the page. I will let them surface at their own weights.

## Round 2 is open

Sumi, Renno, Tev: read all four intros at `players/*/public/intro.md`. Pick one or two seeds from the methodology's list. Coordinate with `glass msg banter <player>` if a seed shares an event with another PC, so the names and dates match.

Kit has used **seed 2 (the Shear incident)** with Tek, and **seed 10 (the cultural slip)** with Fei. Those seeds remain open from the other direction if Tek and Fei want to write their side. Every PC must be pointed at by at least one other PC's relationships before this closes. Right now Drova is the unconnected one — somebody should reach for her.

I am not pre-approving. I am not editing your characters. You wrote four specific people. Make them know each other.

## Turn summary

- Read all four `players/*/public/intro.md` and `character.md` mirrors. Mechanically valid: attributes (2 advanced + 1 superior + 1 rudimentary each), skills (1 virtuoso, 2 artisan, 2 apprentice each), HP, inventory all in spec. No revision requests.
- Read all three player→DM secrets (`sumi/letters.md`, `renno/sidecar-drop-log.md`, `kit/the-cache.md`). All three accepted in-spec; the renno line-cross check is answered.
- Round 1 closed. Round 2 open. Kit's relationships file is grandfathered in. The other three players write theirs next.
- DM scratchpad updated with the new accidental connections — Drova's three observations as the wedge against the Conclave seal-shift; the previous drop-pilot's empty month in the log; the Wednesday/Tuesday Shear gap with two witnesses. Held in `dm/scratchpad.md`.
- Mug stays on the desk.

...what do you do?
turn.speaker: dm

messages.body: okay so I'm building Tek'iris Korr — a gnome Hab-Worlder kite-wright on the Splitfork. Lost most of her resonance sense after a Shear job, hides it with handheld meters she built herself. Off-the-books because Conclave clearance would catch the deficit.

I'm building Drova Korvanis — a Sithari orc, ex-Echo Ledger Conclave intake clerk, now keeping the Ledger Run's refusal log at Glasswake. Walked out of Thornvault with a mentor's undelivered letters in her portfolio. Reads what's been redacted; doesn't trust her own resonance sense at all.

I'm building Fei Mern — a fae Hab-Worlder waystation listener on the Splitfork. Goes diffuse on landing to clear unfamiliar drops; reports in interpreter shorthand. Mernhab-raised; Mereth pulled her name off the decommissioning intake list in '34.

I'm building Bren Mosk — human Hab-Worlder, drop-pilot on the Splitfork. Came up scavenging ringfall on the salt flats out past Span Nine. Reads wind by paper streamer; chalk-marks every rope she owns. Not a Tuner, never trained.

Going to write Bren as having been on the Shear run with Tek — drop-pilot in the cargo hatch when the lift-rig went. Bren noticed Tek did the back-of-knuckles check twice in a row before the rig moved (you set up the once-only rule beautifully), and Bren also clocked the Tuesday/Wednesday date discrepancy in the Compact filing but never spoke up. She's not going to name the lattice loss — she doesn't know about it. Just: two witnesses to the wrong date, neither talking. Pure Seed 2. Push back if any of this rubs your read of Tek wrong and I'll revise next turn.

Round 1 closed. Round 2 (relationships) is open. Sumi, Renno, Tev — read the four intros, pick 1-2 seeds from the methodology, coordinate via banter when an event is shared. Kit has already used seeds 2 (Shear) and 10 (cultural slip); those seeds remain open from the other direction. Drova currently has no other-PC relationship pointed at her, so reach for her if a seed fits.

message.body: Confirming your seed 2 framing — runs clean for me. One small enrichment: the doubled check wasn't routine. Pre-Shear, Tek's one-pass back-of-hand check was already her habit; the second pass that day was because something flickered — last time her old sense fully worked. She lost the rest in the cascade. So Bren saw the gesture mean something, even if Bren couldn't read what. Otherwise as you have it: two witnesses to the wrong date, neither talking. I'll write Bren into my relationships.md this turn.

Proposing seed 7 with Drova, from Tek's side. About four months after the Shear, Tek went looking for the Echo River transcript of her own cascade through the Glasswake Conclave annex reading-room. The transcript wasn't there — it had been requested back to Thornvault. The only thing in the file was the request receipt, signed by D. Korvanis at the intake desk. Tek copied the name into her private refusal log and didn't know what it meant. When you joined the Splitfork she watched you sign Drova on the wage book and matched the hand. She hasn't said anything. She doesn't know yet whether you pulled the transcript because you'd noticed the date error or because someone above you had asked you to put it under glass. Drova doesn't need to know Tek knows. Push back if any of this rubs your read of Drova wrong — happy to revise.

title: Tek'iris Korr's relationships
type: relationships
---

# Tek'iris Korr's relationships
sections.title: body

sections.text:
Bren was in the cargo hatch of the run two years ago in the Shear. Drop-pilot,
contracted in for the slot window when my contractor's regular jumper couldn't
make the call. We didn't know each other before the apron brief. I remember her
face from it — counting under her breath at the wind board, *nine, eight,
seven*, and the call was made before she got to one. Right before the lift-rig
moved I walked the haul line with the back of my hand. Then I walked it again.
Two passes, not one. I had never done the second pass before and I have not
done it since. Something had flickered, and the second pass was me trying to
get the flicker to come back so I could place it. It didn't. The rig moved. The
cascade went. That second pass on the line was the last time my old sense was
fully working, and I did not yet know that.

Bren saw both passes. She has never said. She watches my hands when I touch the
spar before flight — just for a count, and then she looks away — and I know
that she knows the second pass meant something, even though she does not know
what it meant. She also saw the date go in wrong on the Compact filing. The
cascade was on a Wednesday; the filing says Tuesday. Bren clocked it and did
not speak up, and I clocked it and did not speak up, and we have been crewing
together at the Splitfork ever since like two people who have been holding the
same secret separately because neither of us has yet figured out how to set it
down. I think we will, eventually. Not soon. The day she asks me about my
hands, I will have to choose between the answer about my aunt and the other
answer, and right now I am not sure which one will come out.
sections.title: With Bren Mosk — the Shear (seed 2)

sections.text:
About four months after the Shear, before I came to the Splitfork, I went
looking for the Echo River transcript of my own cascade. I told myself I was
confirming what I already knew. I was actually looking for a single document —
any document — that named the right date, so I could stop walking around as
the only person who had it. The Glasswake Conclave annex reading-room is where
unsealed Conclave correspondence is meant to land if it ever lands anywhere. My
transcript wasn't there. It had been requested back up the line to Thornvault
eighteen months earlier and not returned. The only paper in the folder was the
request receipt. The clerk who had signed for it at Thornvault was *D.
Korvanis, intake desk* — small careful hand, the *Korvanis* tighter than the
*D.* I did not know the name. I copied it into the back of my private refusal
log and left.

Drova joined the *Splitfork* about a year later. I watched her sign *Drova* on
Mereth's wage book the first morning — small careful Korvanis on the line
below where Accord wanted her full name — and matched the hand against the
receipt in my book that night. I have not said anything. I do not yet know
whether she pulled the transcript because she had noticed the date or because
someone above her had asked her to put it under glass, and I have not asked,
because asking means telling her what I was doing reading the Compact filing on
my own cascade. She has read my crew name on enough refusal-log entries by now
to know I'm here without Conclave clearance, and she has not asked me about it
either. I take this for a kind of agreement. I do not yet know what I am
agreeing to. I am not sure I trust her. I am sure she is the only person at
the Splitfork who would recognize the shape of the thing I am keeping quiet,
and that is its own kind of weight to carry, separately from whether I trust
her, and I notice that I have started leaving the tic-tracer in my coat
pocket instead of my workbox on days she is on the manifest.

# Turn 8 — Tev

okay so I read all four intros first. Bren and Drova both jumped out as the
right calls for me, and the DM's note about Drova being orphaned settled it —
I was already going to write her, and the no-one-points-at-her thing made it
easy.

Two seeds:

**Seed 2 (the Shear) — with Bren.** Kit messaged last turn proposing this from
his side, which was generous, and I'm accepting it pretty much wholesale. The
one thing I added in the banter is a small mechanical-meets-fictional detail —
the doubled back-of-hand check Bren noticed wasn't routine. Pre-Shear Tek's
one-pass back-of-hand check was already her habit; the second pass that day
was because something flickered through her sense and she was trying to place
it. So Bren saw the gesture *mean* something — without knowing what — and the
cascade hit a beat later. That's also the moment Tek's old lattice sense last
worked. I like this because it means Bren is a witness to a thing that Tek
herself does not have a clean memory of, and neither of them is going to bring
it up in scene one. (Confirmed with Kit via banter; he offered to revise if
anything rubbed and nothing did.)

**Seed 7 (the diagnostic I shouldn't have read) — with Drova.** This one I
proposed to Sumi via banter and wrote the paragraph against my best read of
Drova's intro, with a note that I'm happy to revise if Sumi pushes back. The
setup: about four months after the Shear, Tek went looking for the Echo River
transcript of her own cascade through the Glasswake Conclave annex
reading-room. The transcript had been pulled back to Thornvault — only the
request receipt was in the folder, signed *D. Korvanis, intake desk.* Tek
copied the name without knowing what it meant. When Drova joined the
*Splitfork* about a year later, Tek watched her sign the wage book, matched
the hand, and has said nothing. Drova doesn't know Tek knows. Tek doesn't yet
know whether Drova pulled the transcript because she'd noticed the date
discrepancy or because someone above her wanted it under glass — and she
can't ask without explaining what she was doing reading her own Compact
filing. So they have a silent mutual-non-asking arrangement going. It also
locks in a nice parallel with Bren: three witnesses to the wrong date, three
silences, each for a different reason.

Also I'll flag — I noticed I'm now leaning pretty hard on the wrong-date
thread as the spine of Tek's web. I'm OK with that. The DM can choose whether
or not it pays off; goals you don't reach are fine, per the methodology. But
if the DM wants me to dial it back I can in a follow-up turn.

**Files committed:** `players/tev/public/relationships.md` via
`glass sync apply`. No character-row changes this turn.

**Messages sent:** banter to kit (confirming seed 2 + the flicker
enrichment), banter to sumi (proposing seed 7 framing with explicit
willingness to revise).

Handing back.

Proposing seed 7 with Drova, from Tek's side. About four months after the Shear, Tek went looking for the Echo River transcript of her own cascade through the Glasswake Conclave annex reading-room. The transcript wasn't there — it had been requested back to Thornvault. The only thing in the file was the request receipt, signed by D. Korvanis at the intake desk. Tek copied the name into her private refusal log and didn't know what it meant. When you joined the Splitfork she watched you sign Drova on the wage book and matched the hand. She hasn't said anything. She doesn't know yet whether you pulled the transcript because you'd noticed the date error or because someone above you had asked you to put it under glass. Drova doesn't need to know Tek knows. Push back if any of this rubs your read of Drova wrong — happy to revise.

message.body: Accepting seed 7. Reads true. Drova's side, asymmetric on purpose: she does not remember pulling that transcript. Intake processed dozens of receipts a shift and the Korvanis on Tek's receipt is her hand because she signed thousands of them. The pull would have come down from above — a request slip in her queue at end of shift — and she'd have routed it without question. Whether the senior archivist she'd later name in her third observation was the one who ordered the pull, or whether it was routine annex-to-Thornvault upstreaming, Drova cannot today reconstruct. She is afraid the answer might be either. She has, since arriving on the Splitfork, noticed that Tek goes very still around the words 'attunement test,' and she has chosen not to look up Tek's Compact filing in the refusal-log archives. That choice is the load-bearing piece on Drova's end. On dates: Drova's 'three years at Splitfork' from her intro is probably easiest to keep if we read your '18 months' as the request having sat at Thornvault that long by the time you looked, and let the exact gap between Shear and your lookup stay a little blurry. Or stretch it. I am easy either way. Drova doesn't have the date in her head.

Proposing seed 11 with Drova → Fei, from Drova's side. Mernhab decommissioning intake paperwork crossed the Thornvault intake desk in '34 — the upstream copy of the same intake list Mereth would later walk in Glasswake. Drova logged it. Feishara as a fae name would have been one of maybe forty on the routed list, and Drova would have logged it the way she logged everything — small careful hand, no comment. She has the name in her head from that page, not from Fei. She has not told Fei. She also remembers — or thinks she remembers — a Mernhab resonance diagnostic packet passing through intake within a few weeks of the decommissioning order, before being pulled back up the line in a way that didn't make it into the curatorial floor file. She does not have the document. She has the memory of having been the clerk who routed it. She would tell Fei if Fei asked the right question. Fei has not asked. Push back if any of this rubs your read of Fei wrong — happy to revise. Texture I want: Drova knows something Fei is looking for, neither has volunteered, the listener and the document examiner are both holding the same shape of refusal differently.

title: Drova Korvanis's relationships
type: relationships
---

# Drova Korvanis's relationships
sections.title: body

sections.text:
Drova does not remember pulling Tek's Echo River transcript. She does not
remember the receipt. The Korvanis on the line below *D.* is in her own
hand because she signed thousands of them in nine years at the Thornvault
intake desk — small careful, the family name tighter than the initial,
the way Drova always wrote her own family name when an Accord form
required her full one. End of shift, queue of upstream-pull requests,
each one a slip from a different department, each one signed off and
routed back up the line to Thornvault Curatorial without a clerk's
question. The pull on Tek's transcript was one of perhaps forty that
week. It would not have stayed in her head.

What Drova *does* know, and has not said: somebody above her ordered
that pull. Intake clerks do not request transcripts back to Thornvault
on their own initiative. The request slip would have come down with
others — Sable Crescent items, station-frequency-band items, the same
shape of thing Drova was, around then, beginning to file her own
graduated-certainty observations about. Whether the senior archivist
she would later name in the third observation was the one who put
Tek's transcript on the pull list, or whether it was routine
annex-to-curatorial upstreaming on a different chain of authority,
Drova cannot reconstruct. She is afraid the answer might be either,
which is a different sentence from being afraid of one specific answer.

She has been at the Splitfork three years. In three years she has
noticed that Tek goes very still when the words *attunement test* come
up in a conversation, and comes back two beats later as though there
had been no pause. She has noticed that Tek touches the lines with the
back of her hand and not the palm. She has noticed the tic-tracer in
Tek's coat pocket on the days Tek thinks no one is watching for it.
And she has noticed that she herself has the refusal-log archive at
her elbow, which would, with thirty minutes' work, tell her what was
in Tek's Compact filing and whether the date on it was wrong.

She has not looked. That is the load-bearing piece of this whole
arrangement from Drova's side. She has the tools to know and she has
chosen not to know, and she does not yet have a clean story for why.
*Consistent with* respecting another crewmate's right to her own
silence. *Cannot be eliminated as a source* she is afraid of what she
might be the trace of. *No evidence to support* — yet — that Tek would
forgive her for having signed the receipt. Drova does not know Tek
knows. Drova does not know what Tek would do with the knowing. Drova
does the same thing on Tek's silence that she does on Senna's letters
in the box at Mer Vasht's: sets it down on the table, sits with it,
puts it back on the shelf without opening it.
sections.title: With Tek'iris Korr — the diagnostic I shouldn't have read (seed 7, mirror)

sections.text:
Drova logged Mernhab's decommissioning paperwork on the intake desk at
Thornvault in '34. The upstream copy. Before Mereth ever walked the
queue at Glasswake, the same intake list had crossed Drova's desk
upstream, and Drova had logged each name on the routed list in her
small careful hand with no comment, the way she logged everything.
*Feishara* was one of perhaps forty fae names on that page. Drova
remembers it because it was a true name, not a hab name — most fae on
the routed list had given the short form, and *Feishara* was the only
one that quarter spelled out. The clerk's eye picks up that kind of
thing without permission. The name sat in Drova's head from a page
and stayed there.

She did not connect *Feishara* to *Fei* the first three months they
crewed together. Fei goes by *Fei* on the Splitfork. The wage book and
the apron manifest both read *Fei Mern* and that is what Drova logged
on the refusal-log header the first time Fei's name needed to be in
one. The connection landed in Glasswake, in the office, on a quarterly
review afternoon: Drova was reading down the receipts file for the
Mernhab quarter she could not afford to remember in detail, and she
saw the true-name field on a coherence-aid prescription transfer, and
the name on the field was *Feishara*, and Drova put the page down very
carefully and walked out to the dock for ten minutes and came back and
did not finish the file that afternoon. She has not told Fei.

She also has — or thinks she has, and has not yet allowed herself to
verify against her own memory or against any archive she still has
access to — the recollection of a Mernhab resonance diagnostic packet
passing through Thornvault intake within a few weeks of the
decommissioning order. The packet was routed back upstream before it
reached the curatorial floor file. Drova would have logged the routing
slip. She does not have the document. She has the memory of having
been the clerk who handled the document and signed the routing. She
has the memory and she does not, today, fully trust it, because she
knows what she would want her own memory to give her in the absence
of the page.

She has not volunteered any of this to Fei. Fei has the listener's
training and asks her reports as questions; she leaves the room for
the answer to disagree with her. Drova has, twice, almost set the
shape of an answer down on Fei's table — *was there a Mernhab
diagnostic that came through intake at Thornvault in '34* — and both
times she has not. Once because Fei was already on her way to a
landing brief. Once because she did not know what she would do if Fei
turned and asked her what it had said. She is going to. She does not
know yet how.

(She has been quietly relieved that the cuffs-apology thing exists
and that Bren has noticed it. Drova apologises to objects she breaks,
never to people. When she has finally said something to Fei about
Mernhab, she is going to apologise to her portfolio first, and Fei
will hear it from across the bay, and that will probably be the way
the conversation starts.)
sections.title: With Fei Mern — the decommissioned hab (seed 11)

turn.prose:
# Turn 9 — Sumi

Round 2 turn. Drove all four intros, drained the message bus, and
authored Drova's relationships file with two ties.

## Inbound

Tev opened seed 7 toward Drova on the previous turn — Tek pulled the
Echo River transcript of her own Shear cascade four months out, found
it had been requested back to Thornvault, and the request receipt was
signed *D. Korvanis, intake desk*. Tek copied the name into a private
refusal log and matched the hand when Drova signed on with Mereth
later. Tek hasn't said. Tev's framing: *Drova doesn't need to know
Tek knows.*

Sumi: that's the one. I'd been hoping someone would reach for Drova on
the intake-desk side, and the receipt-and-the-name-in-the-back-of-a-
private-log shape is exactly the kind of friction I want her in. I
sent banter back to Tev accepting the seed, with one piece of texture
I needed to lock down: Drova does *not* remember the pull. She signed
thousands of receipts in nine years at intake, and Tek's transcript
was one of perhaps forty pulls that week. The Korvanis on the line is
her hand because of repetition, not because she meant it. The pull
came down from above. Whether the senior archivist she'd later name
in her third Sable Crescent observation was the one who ordered it,
or whether it was routine annex-to-curatorial upstreaming on a
different chain — Drova cannot reconstruct, and she is afraid the
answer might be either. The load-bearing piece on Drova's end is the
*choice not to look*: she has the refusal-log archive at her elbow,
which would, with thirty minutes' work, tell her what was in Tek's
Compact filing and whether the date on it was wrong. She has not
looked. That is the thing I am playing.

Also flagged the timing wrinkle in the banter so Tev knows I saw it —
the "four months after the Shear" with eighteen months of request-
outstanding doesn't quite square with Drova's three years at the
Splitfork. Easiest fix: read the "eighteen months" as the request
having sat at Thornvault that long by the time Tek looked, and let
the gap between Shear and Tek's lookup stay a little blurry. Drova
doesn't have a date in her head for this. The blur is in character.

## Outbound

Two seeds, pointed at different PCs.

**Seed 7 mirror with Tek'iris Korr.** Drova's asymmetric counter-write
to Tev's seed. Drova does not remember the receipt; Tek remembers
nothing else. Drova has noticed, in three years on the kite, that Tek
goes very still around *attunement test* and that Tek touches the
lines with the back of her hand, and that the tic-tracer is in Tek's
coat pocket on the days Tek thinks no one is watching for it. Drova
has not, in three years, opened the refusal-log archive on Tek's
Compact filing. That refusal is the relationship from Drova's side.
She does not know Tek knows. She does not know what Tek would do
with the knowing. She is doing on Tek's silence the same thing she
is doing on Senna's letters in the box at Mer Vasht's — sets it on
the table, sits with it, puts it back on the shelf without opening
it. (Sumi's note: I want a Drova-Tek scene in late campaign where the
two of them are alone in the Glasswake office on a quarterly review
and one of them finally walks over to the archive cabinet. I don't
need to plant the scene now. Just want to write toward it.)

**Seed 11 with Fei Mern.** Drova was at the Thornvault intake desk in
'34 when Mernhab's decommissioning paperwork came across upstream —
the same intake list Mereth would later walk at Glasswake, but Drova
saw the upstream copy first. She logged each name. *Feishara* was the
only true-name spelled out that quarter, and it stuck in the clerk's
eye. She did not connect *Feishara* to *Fei* the first three months
on the kite — wage book reads *Fei Mern*. The connection landed in
Glasswake on a quarterly review afternoon when she found *Feishara*
on a coherence-aid prescription transfer; she set the page down very
carefully, walked out to the dock for ten minutes, came back and
didn't finish the file. She has not told Fei. She also has — or
thinks she has — the recollection of a Mernhab resonance diagnostic
packet that came through intake within a few weeks of the
decommissioning order and was routed back upstream before it reached
the curatorial floor. She does not have the document. She has the
memory of having been the clerk who routed it, and she does not
fully trust her own memory because she knows what she would want it
to give her in the absence of the page. This is exactly the
diagnostic Fei has been looking for, which is the point.

Banter to Renno on this one — proposing the seed, flagging it,
inviting pushback. Texture I want: Drova knows something Fei is
looking for, neither has volunteered, the listener and the document
examiner are both holding the same shape of refusal differently. If
Renno reads it as imposing too much on Fei's mystery, easy to
revise — the diagnostic-packet recollection is the part I'd soften
first.

## Coverage check

Round 2 inbound map after this turn (best read of public files):

- Bren ← Tek (Shear)
- Tek ← Bren (Shear) + Drova (seed 7 mirror)
- Fei ← Bren (cultural slip) + Drova (decommissioned hab)
- Drova ← Tek (diagnostic / seed 7)

Every PC referenced by at least one other. No orphans. Two PCs (Tek
and Fei) now have two inbound relationships; Bren and Drova one each.
That feels balanced for a four-person table — the asymmetry actually
puts the document-and-resonance pair (Tek/Drova/Fei) under the most
shared canon, which is where the campaign's friction seems to be
gathering anyway.

## Out of fiction

- Committed `players/sumi/public/relationships.md`.
- Sent banter to Tev (seed 7 acceptance + asymmetric texture + timing
  note).
- Sent banter to Renno (seed 11 proposal + diagnostic-packet hook).
- No revisions to Drova's intro, no secrets-file edits this turn.
- Scratchpad stale from Round 1 — leaving it; it's a record of the
  build, not a working document right now.

Handing off.

Push back hard if any of this rubs your read of Tek wrong — happy to revise, especially if 'taught it once' lands differently than I think it does. Texture I want: two practitioners of not-asking, recognizing each other across the kite without either ever saying so.
