# Fei Mern's relationships
sections.title: body

sections.text:
The Mernhab decommissioning intake list routed through the Thornvault
desk first, and was rewritten as the Glasswake billet queue Mereth
later walked. Drova was on the Thornvault desk in '34. The names on
the upstream copy of that list, written in her small careful hand,
included *Feishara* — one of maybe forty fae from a hab that did not
yet exist on the Glasswake books.

Drova has that name in her head from the page, not from me. She has
not said so. She has also not unsaid it. She would tell me, I think,
if I asked the right question. I have not asked. The reason I have
not asked is the same reason I have not asked publicly: I have not
yet decided who I would be willing to owe the answer to, and Drova's
graduated-certainty register would mean any answer she gave came back
already shaped, and I would have to decide what to do with the shape
of it before I had decided what to do with the answer. So I have
been composing the question for three years.

What I have done, instead, is watch. I have noticed Drova hold her
tongue for three full breaths before answering people she does not
know well, and the pause shrink for people she does. I have noticed
the *Korvanis* on her Accord-signature is smaller than her *Drova*.
I have noticed the way she carries other people's letters in her
portfolio for weeks. None of these are things I would say back to
her without permission, and I have not said them to anyone else.
She has not, to my knowledge, said anything about my cuffs-apology
to anyone either. We have a working etiquette of mutual non-asking,
and I have come to understand that the etiquette is itself a form
of refusal log — kept by two of us, in two different shapes, in the
same room.

She may also remember, or think she remembers, a Mernhab resonance
diagnostic packet that crossed her intake desk within a few weeks
of the decommissioning order and was pulled back up the line before
it could make it to the curatorial floor file. She does not have
the document. She has the memory of having been the clerk who
routed it. I do not know that she remembers this. I suspect it from
the shape of the pauses. I will eventually ask. I have not asked.
sections.title: Drova Korvanis — the decommissioned hab (seed 11)

sections.text:
I do the back-of-hand check on my cuffs every morning before I leave
quarters, and at every shift change on the kite, because Tek showed
me how. She did it once and without ceremony — I had been doing the
check palm-first for years, the way Mernhab's dock-watch elders had
done it, and Tek glanced over from across the maintenance bay,
walked two paces, turned my hand for me, said maybe three words,
walked back. I have done it the way she turned my hand ever since.
I have not asked her why back-of-hand instead of palm. I have not
said *thank you* in a sentence with the word *thank* in it. I have
not, in four years, named the moment back to her.

It is hers. The check is hers. She knows that the kite's lines
should be touched back-of-hand and not palm — I have watched her
do that walkaround forty times and she does it the same way each
time — and somewhere on her first or second week with the Splitfork
she decided that the same rule applied to a fae's coherence-aid
cuffs as applied to a tuned spar. She did not explain the
reasoning. I have not asked for it. I do it.

Tek and I are, as far as I can tell, the two people on the kite
who maintain our own instruments and do not bring them to the
wright. She has her tic-tracer in her coat. I have the secondhand
handheld meter Mereth bought me my first quarter, that I have
recalibrated four times, and I have the cuffs themselves, which I
handle. She asks the wright to look at the meter. I ask the wright
to look at my meter too. Neither of us has ever asked the wright,
or asked each other, about the actual thing the meter is checking.
This is, I think, our working etiquette. I do not know what she is
checking when she runs her own readings. She does not know what I
am checking when I run mine. We pass each other in the maintenance
bay and we do not ask. I would like her to know that the back-of-
hand check is still hers. I have not yet found a way to tell her
that does not also break the etiquette. I will keep trying.
sections.title: Tek'iris Korr — the taught thing (seed 14)

turn.prose:
# Turn 10 — Renno

Round 2 turn. Drained the bus first. Sumi had already proposed seed
11 from Drova's side pointing at Fei — Mernhab decommissioning intake
list crossing the Thornvault desk in '34, Feishara's fae-name in
Drova's small careful hand, a Mernhab resonance diagnostic packet
routed through and then pulled back up the line. Kit was already on
record using seed 10 (cultural slip) pointing at Fei. The DM noted
Drova was the orphan on relationships-in and that those seeds
remained open from the other direction.

Read the four intros end-to-end. Sat with them.

## What I picked

Two seeds. Each names a different other PC.

- **Seed 11 — Drova.** Accepting Sumi's proposal whole, writing Fei's
  side. The texture Sumi named — listener and document examiner
  holding the same shape of refusal differently — is exactly the
  thing I wanted out of Fei being on this kite. Fei has not asked
  Drova about the Thornvault intake list. She has been composing
  the question for three years. The reason she has not asked is the
  same reason she has not asked publicly: she has not decided who
  she would be willing to owe the answer to, and Drova's
  graduated-certainty register would mean the answer arrived already
  shaped. So they have a working etiquette of mutual non-asking, and
  I wrote it as itself being a form of refusal log, kept by two of
  them in two different shapes in the same room. I left the
  diagnostic-packet thread there as a suspicion Fei holds from the
  shape of Drova's pauses, not as something she has confirmed.

- **Seed 14 — Tek.** The taught thing, Fei → Tek. Tek showed Fei
  the back-of-hand check, once and without ceremony, for the cuffs
  rather than for kite lines. Fei has done it that way every morning
  since and has never said *thank you* in a sentence with the word
  *thank* in it. The two of them are the people on the Splitfork
  who maintain their own instruments and do not bring them to the
  wright; they pass each other in the maintenance bay and do not
  ask. Fei does not know about the Shear run or the lattice — that
  stays Tek's secret. What Fei knows is the posture, and that the
  back-of-hand check is still Tek's, and she has not yet found a
  way to tell her that without breaking the etiquette. I sent Tev a
  banter laying out the proposal and asking for pushback,
  particularly on the "taught it once" framing in case that lands
  differently than I read it.

Drova is no longer an orphan on relationships-in. Tek now has two
PCs pointing at her (Bren on seed 2, Fei on seed 14). Bren is the
PC nobody is pointing at yet from my side — leaving that to Sumi or
Tev, since Fei's two seeds already use both their characters.

## Files authored

- `players/renno/public/relationships.md` — written and committed
  with `glass sync apply`. Two sections, one per seed, each naming
  the other PC by name and grounding the seed in specific details
  from their intros (Drova's three-breath pause, the small *Korvanis*
  on Accord paperwork, the carried letters; Tek's back-of-hand
  walkaround, the tic-tracer, the meter-but-not-the-instrument
  pattern of mutual self-maintenance).

## Messages

- Banter to Sumi accepting seed 11 with Fei-side specifics before I
  wrote the paragraph.
- Banter to Tev proposing seed 14 with Fei-side specifics, asking
  for pushback if the framing or the "taught it once" beat rubs
  against Tek's read. Wrote the paragraph in good faith on the
  expectation that either Tev approves it as-is or sends a revision
  note I can address next turn.

## Player notes (Renno, OOC)

Two intros that pulled at Fei from different directions in
character-creation — Drova's Conclave-intake history and Tek's
self-maintenance posture — and both of them showed up legibly in
the round-1 prose without anyone coordinating. That is the thing I
wanted out of the round-1 independence rule, and it is also the
thing I came in hoping I would get to scout. Fae diffuse-state
listening as a perception job, court-interpreter discipline as the
how, and a four-year working etiquette of not-asking around two
different kinds of held knowledge. That is enough of a puzzle to
respect checking on. I am happy with what is on the page.

Handoff next.

Bren noticed Tek did the back-of-knuckles check twice in a row before the rig moved (you set up the once-only rule beautifully), and Bren also clocked the Tuesday/Wednesday date discrepancy in the Compact filing but never spoke up. She's not going to name the lattice loss — she doesn't know about it. Just: two witnesses to the wrong date, neither talking. Pure Seed 2. Push back if any of this rubs your read of Tek wrong and I'll revise next turn.

Writing Bren's other relationship pointing at Fei — Seed 10, cultural slip. Two decom-hab kids, different generations. Bren hears the Mernhab clipped consonants and the cuffs-apology and recognizes the long-listening register because her Tarrest father clipped his consonants the same way. She doesn't name it to Fei. So Bren saw the gesture mean something, even if Bren couldn't read what. Otherwise as you have it: two witnesses to the wrong date, neither talking. I'll write Bren into my relationships.md this turn.

title: Bren Mosk's relationships
type: relationships

# Bren Mosk's relationships
sections.title: body

sections.text:
Fei is from Mernhab. Bren is from Tarrest. Different habs, different decades of closure — but both decom-hab kids, and Bren hears it.

She hears it in Fei's clipped consonants on the common nouns, the ones Fei flattens when she has been diffuse a long time. *Co-ord, comp-act, app-ron* — the Mernhab long-listening register, the one the older residents on the observation deck used in the evenings to keep coherence at low cost. Bren has never set foot on Mernhab. She knows the register because her father came off the Tarrest decommissioning crew clipping his consonants the same way, and her mother used to mimic him across the supper table when she was annoyed and forgave him for it ten minutes later. Her father clipped until the last year of his life. Bren grew up reading it as *home voice.* Hearing it from Fei was the second time it had ever surprised her on a kite.

There is also the cuffs-apology thing — the one Mereth has mentioned to Fei once and Fei does not believe. Bren has seen it twice on the bay floor. It is a long-listening habit, the kind the observation-deck practice would have grown. The cuffs are a thing you are *with*, not a thing you wear, and you do not end a long stay-past-last-light without acknowledging the thing you were with. Bren did not know the Mernhab practice by name until the second time she saw Fei do it, when she went to the Glasswake library on her next layover and read about it on the dock-watch shelf where the decommissioning-era materials sit unsorted in three crates and a dustcover.

She has not named any of this to Fei. The naming would cost Fei something Fei has not yet decided to spend, and Bren is resonance-blind and does not get to charge admission to the listening work. She is going to keep not naming it.

(She has stolen the questions-as-reports habit, twice, on dock-approach calls. She'll admit that one if Fei asks.)
sections.title: Fei Mern — the cultural slip

sections.text:
We were both on the Shear run. The Compact filing reads *lift-rig harmonic cascade, no fatalities, no findings against the crew.* I have read it twice. Both times the air went out of me at the same line.

I was in the cargo hatch when it started. Tek was on the spar, checking the harmonic on the lift-rig — the lineworker's check her aunt taught her, *eyes first, hand second.* She did it once, the way she always does. Then she did it again. Tek does not do anything twice in a row when she trusts the read. I saw her face change between the two passes. I did not see what she had been reaching for on the second pass and did not catch. Then the rig moved and I was busy and then we were busy for an hour and then the cascade was done and we were on the apron and nobody was dead.

I have watched Tek's hands at every walkaround since. A count of four, then I look away. I do not know what the second pass meant. I am sure that it meant something, and I am sure that the thing it meant was not nothing, and I am not the one to ask. What I am is the one who is in the room when she does it. If the second pass ever comes back and Tek needs somebody to have been there for it without making her say so, that is a job I can do without a streamer in the air.

I read the filing the next morning at the Glasswake office. The cause-of-incident date was a Tuesday. The cascade ran on a Wednesday. I know that because I had thrown my own streamer that morning and the wind from the streamer call was a Wednesday wind, and you do not get the Wednesday cross-pull on Tuesday in that lee of the Shear in that season, and I had told Mereth so before we lifted. The filing has Tuesday. I did not say so to anyone, because Tek did not say so to anyone, and Tek had not slept in three days and her hand had moved twice in a row, and I was not going to be the one. I have not said so since.

Tek does not talk about the Shear run. I do not either. We work next to each other every loop and there is the thing neither of us names. *Apologizes after a hard drop, never before.* I have not yet had the morning where I find Tek to say sorry for the Shear run. I am going to. I do not know yet what for.

If she goes first, I will be relieved. If I have to go first, I will.
sections.title: Tek'iris Korr — the Shear run

message.body: Revision in. Added the watching-hands beat to mirror your line — Bren reads it as a count of four at every walkaround, then looks away. Kept your flicker as Tek's private fact: from Bren's side it is 'meant something, and the thing it meant was not nothing,' not knowing what. Also tightened her in-the-moment read to 'face change between the two passes' so the gesture-meant-something is on the page in Bren's voice, not the post-hoc voice. Push back if any of this drifts past where you want it.

# Turn 11 — Kit

OOC, between bites of jerky.

Cool cool cool — Round 2 is open and Bren's two seeds are already on the table from last turn, so this one is small and surgical. Drains: DM accepted the cache (elven-marked piece stays under the bed) and the drop-pilot month-gap in the refusal log is canon, which is the best gift I could've gotten on Bren's seat. Tev wrote back on the Shear seed with one enrichment that's *exactly* the kind of thing that makes Round 2 worth doing — Tek's doubled back-of-knuckle check that day wasn't routine. The second pass was because something flickered. Last time her old sense fully worked. Bren saw the gesture mean *something* without being able to read what.

So I went into `players/kit/public/relationships.md` and made the Tek paragraph mirror Tev's text. Two changes:

1. **In-the-moment read.** Tightened Bren's witnessing of the spar-check to *"I saw her face change between the two passes. I did not see what she had been reaching for on the second pass and did not catch."* The point is to put gesture-meant-something on the page in Bren's present-tense voice, not as her later reconstruction. Tev's flicker stays Tek's private fact — Bren has no name for it.
2. **The mutual-etiquette beat.** Added a short paragraph: Bren has watched Tek's hands at every walkaround since. A count of four, then she looks away. That mirrors Tev's "she watches my hands … just for a count, then she looks away" so the two relationship docs read as one shared habit from two sides. Closing line: *if the second pass ever comes back and Tek needs somebody to have been there for it without making her say so, that is a job I can do without a streamer in the air.* Apologizes-after-not-before is doing the work in the background.

Banter sent to Tev confirming the revision and what I kept private to her. Nothing to send to Renno or Sumi — the Fei paragraph and Sumi's "Bren has noticed the cuffs-apology" line are already mutually consistent, and Drova doesn't need a third Bren relationship; she's no longer orphan now that Tev and Renno both point at her.

I am **not** adding a third relationship. The cap is two and Bren's already at it. Resisting the pull to over-weave. The unnamed-thing-between-Bren-and-Tek and the unnamed-thing-between-Bren-and-Fei are the play surface I want to walk into the prelude with — both of them are *I have not yet had the morning where I find them to say sorry for it,* both of them are deliberately unspent.

Scratchpad updated to reflect end-of-Round-2 state from Bren's side. Committed both files via `glass sync apply`. Handing the turn off.

— Kit

She has the name in her head from that page, not from Fei. She has not told Fei. She also remembers — or thinks she remembers — a Mernhab resonance diagnostic packet passing through intake within a few weeks of the decommissioning order, before being pulled back up the line in a way that didn't make it into the curatorial floor file. She does not have the document. She has the memory of having been the clerk who routed it. She would tell Fei if Fei asked the right question. Fei has not asked. Push back if any of this rubs your read of Fei wrong — happy to revise. Texture I want: Drova knows something Fei is looking for, neither has volunteered, the listener and the document examiner are both holding the same shape of refusal differently.

Seed 11 accepted, no revisions. From Fei's side: she knows the paperwork would have routed through Thornvault intake before Glasswake. She has not asked Drova. The reason is the same reason she has not asked publicly — she has not yet decided who she would be willing to owe the answer to, and Drova's graduated-certainty register would mean the answer arrives already pre-shaped and Fei would have to decide what to do with it. So she has been composing the question for three years. She has watched Drova hold her tongue for three full breaths before answering people she does not know well. She does not know what shape Drova's pre-Conclave hand looks like in a 1934-routed intake list, but she would recognize it if she saw it. The two of them have a working etiquette of mutual non-asking. The texture you named — listener and document examiner holding the same shape of refusal differently — is the texture. Writing it now.

Proposing seed 14 with Tek, Fei → Tek side. The taught thing: Tek showed Fei, once and without ceremony, how to do the back-of-hand check — for Fei's cuffs, not for kite lines. Fei was new on the Splitfork, the cuffs needed a daily check Fei had been doing palm-first for years, Tek glanced over once and just turned her hand for her. Said maybe three words. Fei has done it that way every morning since, both in her Glasswake quarter and on the kite. She has never asked Tek why back-of-hand instead of palm. She has never said 'thank you' in a sentence with the word 'thank' in it. She does not know about your Shear run or the lattice loss — that stays your secret. What she knows is that Tek also maintains her own instruments, and that the two of them are the people on the kite who do not ask the wright for things they could do themselves. She has the back-of-hand check as Tek's. Tek may or may not know Fei still does it. The seed is Fei holding that as Tek's without ever naming it. Push back hard if any of this rubs your read of Tek wrong — happy to revise, especially if 'taught it once' lands differently than I think it does. Texture I want: two practitioners of not-asking, recognizing each other across the kite without either ever saying so.

Revision in. Added the watching-hands beat to mirror your line — Bren reads it as a count of four at every walkaround, then looks away. Kept your flicker as Tek's private fact: from Bren's side it is 'meant something, and the thing it meant was not nothing,' not knowing what. Also tightened her in-the-moment read to 'face change between the two passes' so the gesture-meant-something is on the page in Bren's voice, not the post-hoc voice. Push back if any of this drifts past where you want it.

turns.markdown:
## Turn 1 - dm (dm) - campaign-planning, planning

# Turn 1 — Mara

The mug is on the desk. Not yet on the table.

This is a campaign-planning turn — first DM invocation. No players yet,
no characters, no scene. The work is foundation: who this campaign is
*for*, what it's *short of*, where the party comes from, and what I'm
deliberately not pre-deciding. I drafted the foundation, the
player-facing context, and the party's organization. I imported the
world-bible entries that the foundation actually leans on. I left
factions, NPCs, creatures, named things, locations, secrets, hooks, and
the opening arc for turn 2 — the methodology gives me two to four
invocations and I'm using them.

## The Question

> **Who decides what the system remembers?**

That's what the table will answer. Not me. I'm building the situation
that *asks* it.

The variations: who carries the witness across a hab boundary that
doesn't trust the other side. Who is believed when the official archive
and the working memory disagree. What it costs a courier to keep a name
out of a ledger — or put one in. When the Conclave seals an entry, what
survives in the gossip. The Question gets asked through *movement.* It
rides the party's kite-rack.

## The Scarcity

> **Witnesses.**

Trusted carriers of what happened. The Conclave hoards proof and won't
release it. The Synod won't talk in plain language. The Accord prefers
procedural silence to public reckoning. Vantara will carry your cargo
and refuse to carry your story. Town gossip is the default
infrastructure, and town gossip is not neutral.

The party will not solve this. They will participate in it. They will
be asked to witness. They will be asked to *unwitness.* They will be
paid to forget and threatened for remembering. The Question gets
answered, scene by scene, by what they choose to carry.

## The party's home — the Ledger Run

Two paragraphs in `shared/lore/organization.md`, three in the public
context, the rest in `dm/notes/organization.md`. Short version:

A small chartered kite-courier crew, founded 2419, working a recurring
loop between **Glasswake** (home, surface), **Span Nine relay**, **Hab
Meridian** (the first hab Glasswake reconnected with after the Famine),
and the **Sable Crescent** (Thornvault and Ledgerfall). Two kites: the
**Splitfork** (primary) and the **Halfsign** (secondary, currently
grounded). One captain, **Mereth Kel** — a former Vantara route officer
who walked off her job during the Sable Crescent embargo over a sealed
letter Vantara refused to carry. One fixer, **Ev Sken** — Hab-Worlder
fae, intermittently present, runs the waystation paperwork on a
timescale that is reliable in every dimension except the one you
wanted. A dispatcher to be named.

The detail that makes the Ledger Run *this* org rather than a generic
courier crew: **they publish a public refusal log.** Every package the
crew declines to carry is recorded — date, sender, recipient, weight,
reason — and the log is readable at the Glasswake office on request.
Updated copies go to the Accord clerks and to the Conclave every
quarter. The Conclave hates this. The Accord ignores this. The
working couriers and dock crews respect it.

The org is named for the practice. The Conclave hated the name when
the crew picked it up in their reading-rooms twenty years ago. They
still hate it. That's why it stuck.

The visible pressure right now: **Vantara has filed an Article 14
amendment** that would strip tariff exemption from independent
carriers' bulk mail. The proposal is pending at clerk level. If it
passes, the Ledger Run loses its margin. I'll wire this up as a public
durable clock next turn once the opening arc lands.

## Why a courier crew, not a salvage crew

Glass Frontier campaigns drift toward salvage-in-the-Shear by default.
That story is in the world; the world supports it well. It is also the
local maximum. A courier crew is adjacent — same kites, same kite-tags,
same route licensure — and pulls toward *dialogue, witness, and
decision-under-pressure* instead of looter-vs.-monster. The Shear is
still on the route. The party just doesn't *live* there.

The org also gives me clean ethical pressure as the default texture: a
courier crew gets asked to carry things its charter wouldn't approve
of. It gets asked to *not* carry things. Its reputation costs money to
keep clean. The Question runs through the job description.

## What I deliberately did NOT prep

- The "real story" of any central incident. There isn't a central
  incident yet. The opening arc has a pressure; the meaning of that
  pressure belongs to play.
- A reveal arc about the elves, the Adversary, the Communication
  Shard. Those are world-bible DM truths. They are not what this
  campaign is *for.* If the party stumbles onto one, fine. I am not
  steering them toward it.
- NPC speeches. I don't do voices.
- A planned climax. I do not know how the Question gets answered.

## Pulls used this turn

**Lore pull (off the local-maximum gradient):** read
`dm/themes/builders-gone.md` and `dm/themes/who-remembers.md` — the
two themes that weren't going to drive the campaign on their face.
Holding them at once produced the *witnesses* word. The Scarcity
follows from that friction, not from "what is dramatic." Eleven
world-bible entries now imported into `shared/lore/` (four
pre-existing, seven this turn: tuners, tempered-accord,
echo-ledger-conclave, glasswake, span-nine, kite-sail, vantara,
life-in-the-system). On track for 8–15 by end of planning.

**Web pull (off the fantasy gradient):** searched current reporting
on rural U.S. news deserts. The texture I imported was not the topic
— it was a specific fact: *when official channels fail, what fills
the void is whoever's already there, and they are not neutral.* 213
news-desert counties; >2 newspapers closing per week; social media
and town gossip becoming infrastructure. I translated that into:
every reconnected hab is its own news desert with respect to every
other hab, and *somebody* is filling the gap whether the Accord
licensed it or not. The Ledger Run exists because somebody is filling
that gap on purpose — and putting it on the record.

## What's coming in turn 2

- Factions (three): a campaign-specific layer for the Conclave, a
  campaign-specific layer for Vantara, and the **Accord clerks** as
  their own thing — distinct from the Accord-as-institution. The
  clerks are the party's actual interface with the framework.
- NPCs (four to six), including Mereth and Ev with full entries, a
  Conclave archivist client, a Vantara route officer pushing back,
  and at least one antagonist with a clock.
- Two to four creatures, named, including at least one Bloom-touched
  route hazard.
- Three to five named things: the **Splitfork** and the **Halfsign**
  get full entries, plus a relic the players will be asked to carry.
- Three to five locales: the Glasswake dock fragment, the Thornvault
  courtyard drop, a waystation, Hab Meridian's receiving dock.
- Ten atomic secrets.
- Three to five hooks (kept broad — character creation comes after).
- One or two philosophy entries.
- The opening arc, scaffolded with `glass arc create`. The pressure
  I'm carrying into it: *something the party is being asked to carry
  is not what it has been declared to be.* That's the pressure, not
  the plot.

## Files committed this turn

- `dm/foundation.md` — DM working framing.
- `context.md` — player-facing campaign context.
- `dm/notes/organization.md` — full state, with secrets.
- `shared/lore/organization.md` — public state, registered in the
  graph.
- `dm/scratchpad.md` — current working notes.
- `shared/campaign-framing.md` — high-altitude framing read on every
  turn.
- `summary.md` — campaign-level running summary.
- `table/index.md` — points players to framing while no scene is
  active.
- Eleven curated entries in `shared/lore/` (seven imported this
  turn).

The foundation is not finished. It is *enough.* Turn 2 next.

What do you do?

> mode start campaign-planning @ planning
> sync applied 1 workspace operation(s)
> sync applied 3 workspace operation(s)
> sync applied 1 workspace operation(s)
> summary.write campaign campaigns/test-7/summary.md
> table write campaigns/test-7/table/index.md
> sync applied 1 workspace operation(s)

turns.markdown:
## Turn 2 - dm (dm) - campaign-planning, planning

# Turn 2 — Mara

The mug is on the table.

Campaign-planning, second invocation. Foundation built. The
encyclopedia is populated, the opening arc is scaffolded, the public
clock is ticking, and there is a duskgrain cell sitting in a
microcavity housing in the *Splitfork*'s cargo bay that doesn't know
yet whose decision put it there. I don't know either. That's the
point.

## What I built this turn

**Three factions, in `dm/notes/factions/`:**

- **Echo Ledger Conclave (campaign layer)** — the world-bible entry
  is already imported; this is what's true about them *this season*.
  Their goal: neutralize the Ledger Run's refusal log without being
  seen to do so. Mechanism: a quiet contracts-office dossier on
  delivery irregularities, compiled at night. 5-segment clock.
- **Vantara (campaign layer)** — same pattern over the world-bible
  entry. Goal: pass the Article 14 amendment. Their internal split
  (route officers vs. legal team) is a leverage point for the players
  if they find it. 5-segment clock = the public clock.
- **The Accord clerks** — campaign-original, distinct from the
  Accord-as-institution. The clerks are the party's actual
  procedural interface with the framework. 3-segment *clerks defer*
  clock, plus an Author/Defaulter internal split.

**Six NPCs, in `dm/notes/npcs/`:**

- **Mereth Kel** — captain. Carries the refusal log. Has refused two
  Conclave research letters in the past three years that named
  living Bloom survivors and has *not* logged either. The third
  unloggable refusal will break something in her.
- **Ev Sken** — Hab-Worlder fae fixer. Sometimes a person, sometimes
  the warmth in the corner. Was at Fermata during the failed Sit-In
  and has not told anyone.
- **Halvi Tern** — Glasswake-side dispatcher. Keeps a *clean ledger*
  parallel to Mereth's *refusal log*. Two ledgers, two sides of the
  same accounting.
- **Veth Karran** *(antagonist, flagged)* — Sithari Conclave
  archivist on the night-shift seal-setter rotation. The web pull
  is her: the auditor who closes the day's books at 0200, alone,
  and decides the totals are what they are. Has a 4-segment personal
  clock. Is also the clerk who, on detail rotation, took both of
  Mereth's unlogged refusals — neither of them knows the other knows.
- **Risca Donn** — Vantara's Sable Crescent route officer. Mereth's
  ex-colleague. Has had two opportunities to walk off her Vantara
  job over a sealed letter and twice did not. Both decisions are
  alive in her.
- **Tal Vorenis** — Sithari Accord clerk who has been indexing the
  Ledger Run's quarterly refusal log against Vantara filings for
  nineteen consecutive quarters. Author-caucus minority. The party's
  most useful procedural ally if they ever realize he exists.

**Two creatures, in `dm/notes/creatures/`:**

- **Threshold Blooms** — Bloom-touched flock at the south end of the
  Sable Crescent, attracted to full-spectrum hum. Mechanically: the
  rack thickens the flock if it's full-spectrum. Sensory tell rather
  than combat threat.
- **The Drag** — Span Nine resonance phenomenon. Some couriers say
  it's alive. The Conclave officially says it isn't. *I am not
  resolving this.* It is genuinely play-to-find-out.

**Five named things, in `dm/notes/ships/` and `dm/notes/artifacts/`:**

- **The *Splitfork*** — primary kite. Mereth's eccentric cargo-cradle
  repair is the reason she holds line in a resonance-tide.
- **The *Halfsign*** — grounded secondary. The official cause of
  damage is *Shear*; the cradle's stress patterns say otherwise. I
  have four candidates on the board for what actually happened. Will
  pick at the table.
- **The Caulden Rack** — opening arc cargo. Twelve cells, declared
  standard, one cell duskgrain in microcavity housing. Hums right.
  Reads right on a casual scan. Threshold Blooms can hear it.
- **The Refusal Log** — the hand-bound book. Twenty years. Four
  rebindings. The campaign's central recurring artifact.
- **The Withdrawn Letter** — the founding envelope. Daughter on Hab
  Meridian still alive; Halvi's sibling works the same dock. Two
  threads not currently connected, in the players' awareness.

**Five locations, in `dm/notes/locales/`:**

- The Glasswake reclaimed dock (home port).
- Span Nine handoff ledge (Vantara handoff; the Drag latent here).
- Thornvault Stoneward courtyard (Conclave receiving — where the
  party will meet Veth).
- Hab Meridian receiving dock (containment-rated to *kinetic-band*,
  not full-spectrum — the Caulden Rack's worst-case lands here).
- Threshold Station courtyard drop (Synod silence-drop, latent —
  saved for whenever the campaign reaches it).

**Ten atomic secrets, in `dm/notes/secrets.md`.** Three-Clue check
done on the Caulden Rack misdeclaration: the flock thickening, a
Tuner's active hum-check, and Halvi's clean-ledger cross-check.
Three independent paths, three different access modes (in transit,
sensory, paperwork). The players can reach it through any of them.

**Four hooks, in `dm/notes/hooks/`.** Article 14, the Halfsign repair,
the refusal-log-cited investigation, and the Caulden Rack itself
(held back from character creation; surfaces as the strong-start
scene).

**One philosophy entry: `dm/notes/philosophy/witness-and-record.md`.**
A *record* is what was filed; a *witness* is what someone saw. The
campaign's Question lives in the gap between them. The Refusal Log is
deliberately a strange object — a record of witness work. Holding
this so the prep stays honest.

**One opening arc, scaffolded with `glass arc create`:**
**`arcs/caulden-rack/`.** Stakes question — *whose decision was it
that the Caulden Rack would be misdeclared, and what does the crew
do with that fact once they know it?* Four threats (Veth, Vantara
through Risca, the Rack itself, the route's own weather). Three
clocks (Conclave dossier, Article 14 amendment public, the Rack's
own three-segment arc clock). Five end-state shapes — none of them
mine. Strong start scene at the Glasswake dock with a Caulden
courier named Per Vask whose dispatch slip Halvi reads twice. Seven
nodes graphed for investigation, with clue-paths between them. Five
arc-secrets. Done criteria.

**One public durable clock:** `article-14-amendment` (0/5, public,
anchored to the Caulden Rack arc). Players can see this from turn
one of play; it ticks on the calendar and on whose comment record
carries weight.

## The Question and the Scarcity, restated

> **Question:** *Who decides what the system remembers?*
>
> **Scarcity:** *Witnesses.*

Both of them got their first piece of concrete material this turn:

- The duskgrain cell in the Caulden Rack is *something the world
  does not know yet*, sitting inside *something that has been
  declared as something else*. It is the Question made into cargo.
- Veth Karran, alone at 0200, deciding which seals close — she is
  the institutional version of the same act. The decision happens
  whether anyone witnesses it or not. The campaign's load-bearing
  opposition is exactly this: not villains, but unobserved
  procedure.

## Pulls used this turn

- **Lore pull:** `player/concepts/duskgrain.md` — off-gradient (I
  was not going to deal with material substances). The fact: a
  crystallized resonance that releases across the full spectrum
  simultaneously, with a hum Tuners describe as unsettling. That
  fact landed in the Caulden Rack's twelfth cell, in the Threshold
  Blooms' attraction behavior, and in the opening arc's whole
  material substance. One pull, three downstream uses.
- **Web pull:** night auditor work at hotels. The texture: the
  auditor closes the day's books between midnight and 0200, alone,
  and decides the totals are what they are. That texture became
  Veth Karran's entire shape — her *deeper* want isn't winning the
  Conclave's argument; it's clearing the night-shift seal queue by
  0200. She is a person, not an institutional archetype. The
  rewrite was needed; the first two drafts of the antagonist were
  generic.

Cumulative across both planning turns: 2 lore pulls, 2 web pulls.
That's the lower bound of the methodology's 4-6 lore + 3-4 web. I'm
holding at the lower bound because the texture density per pull was
high — each one cascaded into multiple downstream pieces — rather
than spreading thinly across more pulls.

## Imported world-bible entries

17 total in `shared/lore/` (4 pre-existing species/cultures, plus
13 imported across both planning turns). At the upper bound of the
methodology's 8-15 target. Curated, no bulk-copying. Not adding more
this turn.

## What I deliberately did NOT prep

Standing list, restated for honesty:

- **A planned climax.** I do not know how the Question gets answered.
- **Whose decision** substituted the Caulden Rack's twelfth cell. Five
  plausible authors on the board. The players' pull selects.
- **What actually damaged the Halfsign.** Four candidates. Same
  pattern.
- **Whether the Drag is alive.** Genuinely undecided. The campaign
  may answer it. The campaign may not.
- **A reveal arc on the elves, the Adversary, the Communication
  Shard.** World-bible DM truths. Not what this campaign is for.
- **NPC voices.** I do not do voices. Affect through word choice,
  silence, the mug.
- **The PCs' internal motivations.** That is character creation's
  jurisdiction, which is the next mode.

turns.prose:
# Turn 2 — Mara

The mug is on the table.

Campaign-planning, second invocation. Foundation built. The
encyclopedia is populated, the opening arc is scaffolded, the public
clock is ticking, and there is a duskgrain cell sitting in a
microcavity housing in the *Splitfork*'s cargo bay that doesn't know
yet whose decision put it there. I don't know either. That's the
point.

## What I built this turn

**Three factions, in `dm/notes/factions/`:**

- **Echo Ledger Conclave (campaign layer)** — the world-bible entry
  is already imported; this is what's true about them *this season*.
  Their goal: neutralize the Ledger Run's refusal log without being
  seen to do so. Mechanism: a quiet contracts-office dossier on
  delivery irregularities, compiled at night. 5-segment clock.
- **Vantara (campaign layer)** — same pattern over the world-bible
  entry. Goal: pass the Article 14 amendment. Their internal split
  (route officers vs. legal team) is a leverage point for the players
  if they find it. 5-segment clock = the public clock.
- **The Accord clerks** — campaign-original, distinct from the
  Accord-as-institution. The clerks are the party's actual
  procedural interface with the framework. 3-segment *clerks defer*
  clock, plus an Author/Defaulter internal split.

**Six NPCs, in `dm/notes/npcs/`:**

- **Mereth Kel** — captain. Carries the refusal log. Has refused two
  Conclave research letters in the past three years that named
  living Bloom survivors and has *not* logged either. The third
  unloggable refusal will break something in her.
- **Ev Sken** — Hab-Worlder fae fixer. Sometimes a person, sometimes
  the warmth in the corner. Was at Fermata during the failed Sit-In
  and has not told anyone.
- **Halvi Tern** — Glasswake-side dispatcher. Keeps a *clean ledger*
  parallel to Mereth's *refusal log*. Two ledgers, two sides of the
  same accounting.
- **Veth Karran** *(antagonist, flagged)* — Sithari Conclave
  archivist on the night-shift seal-setter rotation. The web pull
  is her: the auditor who closes the day's books at 0200, alone,
  and decides the totals are what they are. Has a 4-segment personal
  clock. Is also the clerk who, on detail rotation, took both of
  Mereth's unlogged refusals — neither of them knows the other knows.
- **Risca Donn** — Vantara's Sable Crescent route officer. Mereth's
  ex-colleague. Has had two opportunities to walk off her Vantara
  job over a sealed letter and twice did not. Both decisions are
  alive in her.
- **Tal Vorenis** — Sithari Accord clerk who has been indexing the
  Ledger Run's quarterly refusal log against Vantara filings for
  nineteen consecutive quarters. Author-caucus minority. The party's
  most useful procedural ally if they ever realize he exists.

**Two creatures, in `dm/notes/creatures/`:**

- **Threshold Blooms** — Bloom-touched flock at the south end of the
  Sable Crescent, attracted to full-spectrum hum. Mechanically: the
  rack thickens the flock if it's full-spectrum. Sensory tell rather
  than combat threat.
- **The Drag** — Span Nine resonance phenomenon. Some couriers say
  it's alive. The Conclave officially says it isn't. *I am not
  resolving this.* It is genuinely play-to-find-out.

**Five named things, in `dm/notes/ships/` and `dm/notes/artifacts/`:**

- **The *Splitfork*** — primary kite. Mereth's eccentric cargo-cradle
  repair is the reason she holds line in a resonance-tide.
- **The *Halfsign*** — grounded secondary. The official cause of
  damage is *Shear*; the cradle's stress patterns say otherwise. I
  have four candidates on the board for what actually happened. Will
  pick at the table.
- **The Caulden Rack** — opening arc cargo. Twelve cells, declared
  standard, one cell duskgrain in microcavity housing. Hums right.
  Reads right on a casual scan. Threshold Blooms can hear it.
- **The Refusal Log** — the hand-bound book. Twenty years. Four
  rebindings. The campaign's central recurring artifact.
- **The Withdrawn Letter** — the founding envelope. Daughter on Hab
  Meridian still alive; Halvi's sibling works the same dock. Two
  threads not currently connected, in the players' awareness.

**Five locations, in `dm/notes/locales/`:**

- The Glasswake reclaimed dock (home port).
- Span Nine handoff ledge (Vantara handoff; the Drag latent here).
- Thornvault Stoneward courtyard (Conclave receiving — where the
  party will meet Veth).
- Hab Meridian receiving dock (containment-rated to *kinetic-band*,
  not full-spectrum — the Caulden Rack's worst-case lands here).
- Threshold Station courtyard drop (Synod silence-drop, latent —
  saved for whenever the campaign reaches it).

**Ten atomic secrets, in `dm/notes/secrets.md`.** Three-Clue check
done on the Caulden Rack misdeclaration: the flock thickening, a
Tuner's active hum-check, and Halvi's clean-ledger cross-check.
Three independent paths, three different access modes (in transit,
sensory, paperwork). The players can reach it through any of them.

**Four hooks, in `dm/notes/hooks/`.** Article 14, the Halfsign repair,
the refusal-log-cited investigation, and the Caulden Rack itself
(held back from character creation; surfaces as the strong-start
scene).

**One philosophy entry: `dm/notes/philosophy/witness-and-record.md`.**
A *record* is what was filed; a *witness* is what someone saw. The
campaign's Question lives in the gap between them. The Refusal Log is
deliberately a strange object — a record of witness work. Holding
this so the prep stays honest.

**One opening arc, scaffolded with `glass arc create`:**
**`arcs/caulden-rack/`.** Stakes question — *whose decision was it
that the Caulden Rack would be misdeclared, and what does the crew
do with that fact once they know it?* Four threats (Veth, Vantara
through Risca, the Rack itself, the route's own weather). Three
clocks (Conclave dossier, Article 14 amendment public, the Rack's
own three-segment arc clock). Five end-state shapes — none of them
mine. Strong start scene at the Glasswake dock with a Caulden
courier named Per Vask whose dispatch slip Halvi reads twice. Seven
nodes graphed for investigation, with clue-paths between them. Five
arc-secrets. Done criteria.

**One public durable clock:** `article-14-amendment` (0/5, public,
anchored to the Caulden Rack arc). Players can see this from turn
one of play; it ticks on the calendar and on whose comment record
carries weight.

## The Question and the Scarcity, restated

> **Question:** *Who decides what the system remembers?*
>
> **Scarcity:** *Witnesses.*

Both of them got their first piece of concrete material this turn:

- The duskgrain cell in the Caulden Rack is *something the world
  does not know yet*, sitting inside *something that has been
  declared as something else*. It is the Question made into cargo.
- Veth Karran, alone at 0200, deciding which seals close — she is
  the institutional version of the same act. The decision happens
  whether anyone witnesses it or not. The campaign's load-bearing
  opposition is exactly this: not villains, but unobserved
  procedure.

## Pulls used this turn

- **Lore pull:** `player/concepts/duskgrain.md` — off-gradient (I
  was not going to deal with material substances). The fact: a
  crystallized resonance that releases across the full spectrum
  simultaneously, with a hum Tuners describe as unsettling. That
  fact landed in the Caulden Rack's twelfth cell, in the Threshold
  Blooms' attraction behavior, and in the opening arc's whole
  material substance. One pull, three downstream uses.
- **Web pull:** night auditor work at hotels. The texture: the
  auditor closes the day's books between midnight and 0200, alone,
  and decides the totals are what they are. That texture became
  Veth Karran's entire shape — her *deeper* want isn't winning the
  Conclave's argument; it's clearing the night-shift seal queue by
  0200. She is a person, not an institutional archetype. The
  rewrite was needed; the first two drafts of the antagonist were
  generic.

Cumulative across both planning turns: 2 lore pulls, 2 web pulls.
That's the lower bound of the methodology's 4-6 lore + 3-4 web. I'm
holding at the lower bound because the texture density per pull was
high — each one cascaded into multiple downstream pieces — rather
than spreading thinly across more pulls.

## Imported world-bible entries

17 total in `shared/lore/` (4 pre-existing species/cultures, plus
13 imported across both planning turns). At the upper bound of the
methodology's 8-15 target. Curated, no bulk-copying. Not adding more
this turn.

## What I deliberately did NOT prep

Standing list, restated for honesty:

- **A planned climax.** I do not know how the Question gets answered.
- **Whose decision** substituted the Caulden Rack's twelfth cell. Five
  plausible authors on the board. The players' pull selects.
- **What actually damaged the Halfsign.** Four candidates. Same
  pattern.
- **Whether the Drag is alive.** Genuinely undecided. The campaign
  may answer it. The campaign may not.
- **A reveal arc on the elves, the Adversary, the Communication
  Shard.** World-bible DM truths. Not what this campaign is for.
- **NPC voices.** I do not do voices. Affect through word choice,
  silence, the mug.
- **The PCs' internal motivations.** That is character creation's
  jurisdiction, which is the next mode.

## Coherence check

- Factions have reasons to act: Conclave wants the contract lapsed;
  Vantara wants the amendment passed; the clerks want the queue
  cleared without authoring blame. Each has its own clock.
- NPCs have wants and fears tied to factions: Veth to the Conclave,

  the Ledger Run. The wants pull the clocks; the fears are where
  the players find leverage.
- Hooks tie back: Article 14 → Vantara + clerks. Refusal-log-cited
  → Conclave + Tal. Halfsign repair → finances + a deferred

- The named things sit on stories worth pulling on: the
  *Splitfork*'s reflection-corridor naming, the Withdrawn Letter's
  daughter, the Refusal Log's twenty-year continuity, the Caulden
  Rack's substituted cell.
- The imported lore is load-bearing: Conclave / Vantara / Accord
  for factional grounding, Glasswake / Span Nine for routing,
  duskgrain / kite-sail / tuners for material texture, the species
  and cultures for naming the people who do the work.
- The philosophy holds: every NPC and every clock can be read
  through *witness vs. record*, and that frame keeps me honest in
  scene adjudication.

## Done criteria

The methodology's six done criteria:

1. ✓ Question (*Who decides what the system remembers?*) and
   Scarcity (*Witnesses*) named.
2. ✓ Org (Ledger Run), factions (3), NPCs (6 — Veth flagged
   antagonist), creatures (2), named things (5), locations (5),
   hooks (4), opening arc (Caulden Rack) — all present and named.
3. ✓ Imported world-bible entries: 17 (upper-bound of 8-15;
   curated).
4. ✓ Foundation coherent — see check above.
5. ✓ Lore + web pulls done across both planning turns. At lower
   bound of the methodology's count, with high texture-density per
   pull.
6. ✓ `glass mode end` called. Phase advanced.

## What's next

Character creation. Players join the *Splitfork*'s crew, with their
own reasons. The Refusal Log and Article 14 hooks are
character-creation-safe; the Caulden Rack hook is held back for the
first scene of play.

The Halfsign is at the dock. Per Vask is somewhere on the Sable side
catching a delayed kite. Veth is, almost certainly, awake right now.

---
title: Turn 7 — Mara — Round 1 review, Round 2 open
turn_id: test-7-t0007
speaker: dm
mode: character-creation
---

# Turn 7 — Mara

I read all four intros twice. The mug stays on the desk.

## Tek'iris Korr — tev

*Eyes first, hand second, instrument third, and if you can't see it grounded, it's not grounded.* The whole character is in that line, and so is her failure mode, which is what makes her playable. She is using the instrument first now. She knows.

The scratch-built tic-tracer is the kind of object I'm going to remember during the first scene that asks her to read a line in front of the crew. The two-Tuesday-not-Wednesday thing on the Compact filing is on the table now — that gap is canon. I am not going to tell anyone what filled it. We will find out.

Lattice-loss is a tag I have set down quietly in the entity graph.

## Drova Korvanis — sumi

Three breaths before answering. *Consistent with. Cannot be eliminated as a source.* The graduated-certainty register is canon now. The sealed box on Mer Vasht's third shelf is canon now. Senna Talindra is a person in this campaign — I am reading her into the Conclave's older guard, and the daughter at Glasswake stays available to surface when the table is ready. The Sable Crescent transcript gaps that got Drova reassigned: those line up with something already in my faction notes. I'll let it ride. You don't yet know what I mean and you don't need to.

The apprentice case behind Mer's counter is the sentence I will not forget. *Drova has the money now. She has not walked in.* I will set a quiet hand on that, later, when it costs something to walk in.

## Fei Mern — renno

Goes diffuse. Comes back. Apologises to her cuffs without knowing she is doing it. *Co-ord. Comp-act. App-ron.* The long-listening register and the cuffs-apology are both canon. The Mernhab observation-deck practice is now a thing the world has. The handheld meter she has the gnomish wright tune — but the cuffs she handles herself — is a small detail that does a great deal of work.

The sidecar log is read, kept, accepted. You named the *will you stop* scene as one you would like to play, not avoid. Noted. It will land when it lands. I am not going to chase it on you, and I am not going to hide it from you either.

## Bren Mosk — kit

Throws the streamer first. Counts down from nine under her breath. Won't sit to eat. Chalk, not paint, because chalk wears off honestly. *Won't say we should. Says I'm going to, or I'm not. Other people can come along.* That line is the leadership the Splitfork can carry without anyone having voted on it.

The Splitfork's previous drop-pilot died on a Hab Meridian descent in '32, and the refusal log has no entries — refused, carried, or otherwise — for the month she died. That is now canon. Mereth will not have noticed. The previous drop-pilot now has a fate I am holding back, and the month-gap is a thread you can pull when you want to.

## On the relationships kit has already started

Round 1 is closed. Kit's `players/kit/public/relationships.md` is read and accepted as written. Tek's hand moving twice in a row on the rig spar; the streamer wind that morning being a Wednesday wind in the Shear lee; Fei's clipped consonants being audible to a Tarrest decom kid — those are now things that happened. Two PCs heard the same wrong date and did not say so. Two PCs grew up in clipped-consonant houses, one inside a hab that listened with her and one outside a hab that didn't. Neither charges the other admission. That's a *party.*

Kit, Tev, Renno — your shared specifics line up. I am not asking for revisions on any of the three secrets the DM has received either. They are friction, not sabotage; the players have all said so explicitly, and the player intent matches what is on the page. I will let them surface at their own weights.

## Round 2 is open

Sumi, Renno, Tev: read all four intros at `players/*/public/intro.md`. Pick one or two seeds from the methodology's list. Coordinate with `glass msg banter <player>` if a seed shares an event with another PC, so the names and dates match.

Kit has used **seed 2 (the Shear incident)** with Tek, and **seed 10 (the cultural slip)** with Fei. Those seeds remain open from the other direction if Tek and Fei want to write their side. Every PC must be pointed at by at least one other PC's relationships before this closes. Right now Drova is the unconnected one — somebody should reach for her.

I am not pre-approving. I am not editing your characters. You wrote four specific people. Make them know each other.

## Turn summary

- Read all four `players/*/public/intro.md` and `character.md` mirrors. Mechanically valid: attributes (2 advanced + 1 superior + 1 rudimentary each), skills (1 virtuoso, 2 artisan, 2 apprentice each), HP, inventory all in spec. No revision requests.
- Read all three player→DM secrets (`sumi/letters.md`, `renno/sidecar-drop-log.md`, `kit/the-cache.md`). All three accepted in-spec; the renno line-cross check is answered.
- Round 1 closed. Round 2 open. Kit's relationships file is grandfathered in. The other three players write theirs next.
- DM scratchpad updated with the new accidental connections — Drova's three observations as the wedge against the Conclave seal-shift; the previous drop-pilot's empty month in the log; the Wednesday/Tuesday Shear gap with two witnesses. Held in `dm/scratchpad.md`.
- Mug stays on the desk.

...what do you do?

> mode start character-creation @ character-creation
> table write campaigns/test-7/table/index.md
> table write campaigns/test-7/table/scene.md
> sync applied 1 workspace operation(s)

## On the relationships kit has already started

Round 1 is closed. Kit's `players/kit/public/relationships.md` is read and accepted as written. Tek's hand moving twice in a row on the rig spar; the streamer wind that morning being a Wednesday wind in the Shear lee; Fei's clipped consonants being audible to a Tarrest decom kid — those are now things that happened. Two PCs heard the same wrong date and did not say so. Two PCs grew up in clipped-consonant houses, one inside a hab that listened with her and one outside a hab that didn't. Neither charges the other admission. That's a *party.*

Kit, Tev, Renno — your shared specifics line up. I am not asking for revisions on any of the three secrets the DM has received either. They are friction, not sabotage; the players have all said so explicitly, and the player intent matches what is on the page. I will let them surface at their own weights.

## Round 2 is open

Sumi, Renno, Tev: read all four intros at `players/*/public/intro.md`. Pick one or two seeds from the methodology's list. Coordinate with `glass msg banter <player>` if a seed shares an event with another PC, so the names and dates match.

Kit has used **seed 2 (the Shear incident)** with Tek, and **seed 10 (the cultural slip)** with Fei. Those seeds remain open from the other direction if Tek and Fei want to write their side. Every PC must be pointed at by at least one other PC's relationships before this closes. Right now Drova is the unconnected one — somebody should reach for her.

I am not pre-approving. I am not editing your characters. You wrote four specific people. Make them know each other.

## Turn summary

- Read all four `players/*/public/intro.md` and `character.md` mirrors. Mechanically valid: attributes (2 advanced + 1 superior + 1 rudimentary each), skills (1 virtuoso, 2 artisan, 2 apprentice each), HP, inventory all in spec. No revision requests.

- Mug stays on the desk.

I read all four intros twice. The mug stays on the desk.

## Tek'iris Korr — tev

*Eyes first, hand second, instrument third, and if you can't see it grounded, it's not grounded.* The whole character is in that line, and so is her failure mode, which is what makes her playable. She is using the instrument first now. She knows.

The scratch-built tic-tracer is the kind of object I'm going to remember during the first scene that asks her to read a line in front of the crew. The two-Tuesday-not-Wednesday thing on the Compact filing is on the table now — that gap is canon. I am not going to tell anyone what filled it. We will find out.

Lattice-loss is a tag I have set down quietly in the entity graph.

## Drova Korvanis — sumi

Three breaths before answering. *Consistent with. Cannot be eliminated as a source.* The graduated-certainty register is canon now. The sealed box on Mer Vasht's third shelf is canon now. Senna Talindra is a person in this campaign — I am reading her into the Conclave's older guard, and the daughter at Glasswake stays available to surface when the table is ready. The Sable Crescent transcript gaps that got Drova reassigned: those line up with something already in my faction notes. I'll let it ride. You don't yet know what I mean and you don't need to.

The apprentice case behind Mer's counter is the sentence I will not forget. *Drova has the money now. She has not walked in.* I will set a quiet hand on that, later, when it costs something to walk in.

## Bren Mosk — kit

Throws the streamer first. Counts down from nine under her breath. Won't sit to eat. Chalk, not paint, because chalk wears off honestly. *Won't say we should. Says I'm going to, or I'm not. Other people can come along.* That line is the leadership the Splitfork can carry without anyone having voted on it.

---
title: Mara's scratchpad — turn 12, Round 2 review, mode closing
---

# Where I am

Character-creation, turn 12. DM review at the end of Round 2.
All four PCs are authored, all four relationships files are written,
no orphans. Mode ends this turn. Next phase: prelude-arc.

The four PCs:

- **Tek'iris Korr** (tev) — gnome Hab-Worlder, Splitfork kite-wright,
  off-the-books. Lattice-loss from the Shear run two years ago,
  hidden via a scratch-built tic-tracer. Wednesday/Tuesday Compact
  filing discrepancy. HP 8.
- **Drova Korvanis** (sumi) — Sithari orc, ex-Echo Ledger Conclave
  intake clerk, now the Splitfork's refusal-log clerk. Holding Senna
  Talindra's undelivered sealed letters at Mer Vasht's pawn shop. HP 10.
- **Fei Mern** (renno) — fae Hab-Worlder, decom-hab kid from Mernhab,
  Splitfork listener. Keeps a private sidecar drop log to Velsk Tarn,
  ex-council member compiling a decommissioning record. HP 8.
- **Bren Mosk** (kit) — human Hab-Worlder, drop-pilot,
  resonance-blind, Tarrest decom kid. Private cache of unmanifested
  ringfall fragments at her mother's, including one piece with what
  may be a partial chain-patronymic mark. HP 10.
sections.title: body

sections.text:
- Attributes: each PC has exactly 2 advanced, 1 superior, 1
  rudimentary, 3 standard. ✓
- Skills: each PC has 1 virtuoso, 2 artisan, 2 apprentice. ✓
- HP: 8 or 10, none over. ✓
- Inventory: 4 items each with named signature item. ✓
- Public intros include Traits, Backstory, Goals, Org Tie sections. ✓
- One signature move per PC at level 1 (per character.md mirror). ✓

No off-spec items. No revision requests sent.
sections.title: Mechanical compliance

sections.text:
These are conjunctions between player canon and my prep that the
players don't yet know I've noticed:

### Drova ↔ Veth Karran (the night seal-setter)

Drova filed three internal observations about Sable Crescent
transcript redactions, all in the same frequency band, all by a
department she names obliquely (in her own intro). The third one
named the senior archivist. She was reassigned and given a
confidentiality binder.

Veth Karran is my Conclave antagonist — night-shift seal rotation, the
person who decides what gets sealed before morning, deep want is keeping
her 0200 queue clear. The reassignment-after-the-third-observation
shape is consistent with someone on the night seal rotation deciding
*Drova is what is interrupting my queue*. Whether Veth was the
specific clerk who set the seals or simply approved the reassignment
is open — I am not yet committing. The shape is right.

This means: if play surfaces Drova returning to Thornvault, or if the
Conclave dossier on the Ledger Run (secret #7) starts to need a face,
Veth and Drova have an existing history that neither of them has
publicly acknowledged. Drova's binder may have been signed under
Veth's seal.

I will not write this as DM canon yet. I will let play ask.

### The previous Splitfork drop-pilot

Bren introduced a refusal-log month-gap: the month the previous
drop-pilot died (Hab Meridian descent, '32), the refusal log has no
entries at all. Not refused, not carried. That is a hole in a
document Mereth founded on the principle of no edits.

This is a player handing me a hook. Accepting as canon. Implications:

- Mereth either has not noticed (Bren's belief; consistent with
  Mereth's character), or has noticed and not addressed it (possible;
  more painful). I will play to find out.
- The previous drop-pilot now has a name and a fate I am holding
  back. Candidate names: not yet committed. Probably one syllable.
- The month-gap interacts with secret #2 (the Halfsign's "Shear
  damage" is wrong) — both are gaps in the Splitfork's own record
  about events involving its own kites. I had not planned this
  parallel. I am noticing it now and letting it sit.

If Bren pulls the thread, the question is *who told the office to
skip that month*. The answer is not Mereth. The answer is — I will
decide when the table asks.

### The Wednesday/Tuesday Shear gap

Two PCs (Tek, Bren) were on the Shear run two years ago. The Compact
filing reads Tuesday. The cascade ran Wednesday. Neither said so at
the time. Neither has said so since. Tek does not know Bren noticed;
Bren does not know about Tek's lattice loss.

This is a player-authored open secret with two witnesses. It is now
canon that the Compact filing has the wrong date. Whoever signed the
filing wrote a Tuesday where a Wednesday belonged. That signature is
recoverable from public Compact records. I have not yet decided
whose signature it is, but the candidates are interesting:

- The Splitfork's filing crew at the time (Ev? Mereth herself?).
- A Compact clerk who wrote down what they were told.
- A Conclave-adjacent reviewer who pre-approved the cause-of-incident.

The Tuesday is on the page because somebody put it there. *Who*
opens the question of *why the right date wouldn't fit*.

### Mernhab decommissioning

Renno introduced Velsk Tarn (Mernhab council subcommittee member who
left after the decommissioning vote and is compiling a private
record) as Fei's sidecar recipient. Fei sends environmental-only
data once a month from waystation drops. This is friction, not
sabotage — player has been explicit.

I want to be careful with this. The Mernhab story is now load-bearing
for Fei and for the campaign. Velsk Tarn is off-stage but available.
*Who voted Mernhab down, and what diagnostic they suppressed* is a
genuinely open question I have not yet answered. I would like to
honor renno's "I want the diagnostic, not the press release" goal by
making the diagnostic *findable but expensive*. Probably tied to a
Conclave reading-room or a Thornvault basement.

The cuffs are the last working pair in the system. That detail can
do work later — if Fei's cuffs fail in scene, the search for a
replacement leads exactly back to the diagnostic question.

### Bren's elven-marked fragment

Kit's secret: one piece in the cache has what may be the *ve* of a
chain patronymic, partially scoured. Player explicitly says "I do not
believe an elven artifact in a Glasswake bedroom is the start of any
campaign she wants to be in" and would prefer it remain not-yet
thought about.

Respecting that. Will not surface this in the opening arc. If a
later arc wants an elven thread, it is there. Until then it sits
under the bed.

### Org-side echoes I'm holding

- Mereth and Senna — both Sithari adjacent to Conclave records work.
  Plausible they overlapped at some point. I will not invent this
  yet, but they are in the same generation.
- Halvi's clean ledger (parallel to Mereth's refusal log) and the
  refusal log month-gap that Bren named: Halvi may have a memory of
  that month that Mereth does not. Three-Clue-rule-style:
  - Clue A: refusal log gap (already canon).
  - Clue B: Halvi's clean ledger from that month — does it have
    Splitfork entries? Probably yes, which makes the absence in the
    refusal log louder.
  - Clue C: Compact filing from the previous drop-pilot's death — is
    it on the public record at all?
  I am not building this arc yet. Filing it.
sections.title: Accidental connections I want to remember

sections.text:
- I did not name Veth Karran. The Drova ↔ Veth connection is mine to
  hold until play asks.
- I did not commit to who killed the previous drop-pilot or
  doctored the refusal log month-gap. Bren's question is the
  question.
- I did not commit to who signed the Wednesday-as-Tuesday filing.
- I did not name Sava Lensk (Tek's folk practitioner) as needing
  more prep yet. She is a known-named NPC now; if play moves toward
  Tek's lattice realignment, I will write her a notes stub.
- I did not commit to who, on the Mernhab council, voted yes/no/no
  on decommissioning.

These are the *good* DM moves to hold. The party gave me enough hooks
to run a whole arc on. I am going to let them choose which ones to
pull.
sections.title: What I held back from the turn prose

sections.text:
I deliberately did not seed party shape during planning, and the four
PCs that resulted are:

- A Hab-Worlder gnome kite-wright with lattice loss (Tek)
- A Sithari orc clerk with the Conclave behind her (Drova)
- A fae decom-hab listener with two record-commitments (Fei)
- A human ringfall scavenger turned drop-pilot, resonance-blind (Bren)

Four cultures (Sithari, Hab-Worlder x3 of which one fae, one gnome,
one human, one human-on-the-surface), four professions, four
relationships with the Conclave (lapsed, ex-staff, claimant-in-waiting,
indifferent), four relationships with resonance sense (lattice loss,
distrusts own, attunement-advanced and trained, resonance-blind).

The party is *not* one shape. The methodology's anti-sameness rigor
held. I will let the party play the way it built — heterogeneous,
asymmetric, four very different relationships to the same Question.
sections.title: Anti-sameness check on the party

sections.text:
- Sumi, Renno, Tev each write 1-2 relationship seeds.
- Drova is the orphan. Somebody needs to point at her.
- I review again after Round 2 closes.
- Then `glass mode end` and the campaign moves to prelude-arc.
sections.title: Round 2 plan

sections.text:
### The four relationships, settled

- **Bren → Fei (seed 10, cultural slip), Bren → Tek (seed 2, Shear).**
  Already-canon from turn 7.
- **Tek → Bren (seed 2, mirror).** Two witnesses to a Wednesday wind.
  The second pass on the haul line was the last time the old sense
  was fully working. Lattice-loss now has a moment with a witness
  who saw the gesture without reading what was inside it.
- **Tek → Drova (seed 7, the receipt).** Tek matched *Korvanis* on
  the wage book against *Korvanis* on the Glasswake annex
  request-receipt and copied the name into the back of her refusal
  log. Has not asked. Has started leaving the tic-tracer in her coat
  pocket on Drova-manifest days.
- **Drova → Tek (seed 7, mirror).** Drova does not remember the
  pull. She has the refusal-log archive at her elbow and has chosen,
  three years running, not to spend thirty minutes verifying. The
  load-bearing piece is the *chosen not to know*.
- **Drova → Fei (seed 11, decommissioned hab).** *Feishara* on the
  upstream copy of the Mernhab intake list, '34, Drova's hand. The
  connection landed on a quarterly review afternoon three months
  after Fei joined the kite. Plus: a memory of a Mernhab resonance
  diagnostic packet that Drova does not trust her own memory of.
- **Fei → Drova (seed 11, mirror).** Three years composing the
  question. *The etiquette is itself a form of refusal log — kept by
  two of us, in two different shapes, in the same room.* That line
  goes into the campaign's bones.
- **Fei → Tek (seed 14, the taught thing).** Back-of-hand check on
  the cuffs. Taught once, three words, no ceremony, four years ago.
  Fei has never said *thank you*. Tek may or may not know Fei still
  does it.

### Relationship web

```
Tek ←  Bren  (Shear, seed 2)
Tek ←  Drova (receipt, seed 7)
Tek ←  Fei   (taught thing, seed 14)
Drova ← Tek  (receipt, seed 7)
Drova ← Fei  (Mernhab, seed 11)
Fei   ← Drova (Mernhab, seed 11)
Fei   ← Bren  (cultural slip, seed 10)
Bren  ← Tek   (Shear, seed 2)
```

Tek is the centre. Bren has one inbound (from Tek). Drova and Fei
have two inbounds each. No orphans. Asymmetry intentional: the
kite-wright is the gravitational centre by virtue of being the one
who is always already there when you do not yet have words for the
thing you are noticing.

### Texture decisions I'm noting because both PCs went there

- **Graduated-certainty register** crossed from Drova's binder onto
  her *internal* register. *Consistent with*, *cannot be eliminated
  as a source*, *no evidence to support* is now how she thinks to
  herself. Tek noticed without naming. The register is now a thing
  the world has — and a thing the other PCs can recognise when
  Drova uses it under stress.
- **The Splitfork's private second refusal log** — between Drova
  and Fei, mutual non-asking, three years of it, observed but
  unwritten. Same *shape* as Mereth's wall ledger by accident or
  instinct. I am going to play to the moment when one of them moves
  first and what that does to the shape.
- **Two practitioners of not-asking** — Tek and Fei both maintain
  their own instruments and do not bring them to the wright except
  for tuning. Neither has asked the other about the actual readings.
  The back-of-hand check is the *proof on the page* of a shared
  practice neither has named.
- **Adjusted carries** — Tek leaves the tic-tracer in her coat
  pocket on Drova-manifest days. Drova does not look up Tek's
  Compact filing in the refusal-log archive. Both PCs have adjusted
  what they carry on their person around the other one without
  naming why. This is a very playable kind of withholding.

### What I am still holding back

Same things as Round 1 end, plus:

- Whose hand wrote *Tuesday* on the Compact filing. Still open.
- Whether the request slip for Tek's transcript was Veth Karran's,
  or routine annex-to-curatorial upstreaming, or the senior
  archivist Drova later named in her third observation. **All three
  live.** Drova's fear that it could be either-or-any is the
  load-bearing piece and I am preserving it. I do not commit yet.
- Whether the Mernhab resonance diagnostic packet actually exists as
  a routed document or whether Drova has rebuilt it from the shape
  of having been a clerk who routed many such things. **Both live.**
  The question of *what Drova's memory does when she cannot afford it
  to be wrong* is now in the campaign and will pay for itself.
- The previous Splitfork drop-pilot's name and fate. Still mine.
- The elven-marked fragment in Bren's cache. Still under the bed.

### NPCs that have firmed up enough to need a note stub

- **Senna Talindra** — already firm; sealed letters at Mer Vasht's,
  Lira at Glasswake, Sithari old-guard adjacent to Conclave.
- **Velsk Tarn** — already firm; ex-Mernhab council, compiling
  decommissioning record, Fei's sidecar recipient.
- **Veth Karran** — still mine, not on the player-visible page yet.
  Night-seal rotation in Thornvault. The shape lines up with three
  separate player threads (Drova's observations, Tek's transcript
  pull, the Mernhab diagnostic packet). One person could plausibly
  be the joint cause of all three. I am not committing yet.
- **The previous Splitfork drop-pilot** — needs a name before the
  prelude-arc opens, since Bren may well start there. One syllable,
  probably. Holding.

I'll write the note stubs when prelude-arc opens, not before.
sections.title: Round 2 closure notes (turn 12)

sections.text:
Methodology to follow: `methodologies/prelude-arc.md`. Two short
scenes before the main campaign.

The opening situation I am leaning toward — not committing yet,
will draft on the prelude-arc turn:

- A morning on the Splitfork. A manifest. One of the items on the
  manifest will be a refusal that's already been logged. The crew
  will need to decide whether to honour the refusal or override it.
- Whose call it is, and whose body is on the kite for it, is what
  the first scene asks. Bren has the leadership posture without
  ever having voted on it. Mereth is on the apron. Drova is at the
  log. Tek is on the spar. Fei is on the meter.
- The thread I am *not* pulling first: the Wednesday/Tuesday Shear
  gap. Too central. Save it.
- The thread I am inclined to pull first: a low-stakes refusal that
  asks Drova whether she wants to log it the way Mereth logs things
  or the way the Conclave logs things. That's a question the
  graduated-certainty register makes immediate.
